test_cuda_event_reconstruct(self):\n412:         def fn(x):\n413:             e = torch.cuda.Event()\n414:             x = torch.mul(x, 5)\n415:             x = torch.add(x, 2)\n416:             return x, e\n417: \n418:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), device=\"cuda\")\n419:         ref = fn(x)\n420:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n421:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n422:         res = opt_fn(x)\n423:         self.assertEqual(ref[0], res[0])\n424:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n425:         self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 3)\n426: \n427:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n428:     def test_cuda_event_across_graph_break(self):\n429:         def fn(x):\n430:             e = torch.cuda.Event()\n431:             e.record()\n432:             x = torch.mul(x, 5)\n433:             x = torch.add(x, 2)\n434: \n435:             print(\"foo\")\n436: \n437:             torch.cuda.current_stream().wait_event(e)\n438:             x = torch.add(x, 1)\n439:             x = torch.cos(x)\n440:             return x, e\n441: \n442:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), device=\"cuda\")\n443:         ref = fn(x)\n444:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n445:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n446:         res = opt_fn(x)\n447:         self.assertEqual(ref[0], res[0])\n448:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n449:         self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 9)\n450: \n451:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n452:     def test_cuda_event_created_outside_of_graph(self):\n453:         user_stream = torch.cuda.Stream()\n454:         event = torch.cuda.Event()\n455:         foo = torch.empty((2, 2), device=\"cuda\")\n456: \n457:         def func(foo):\n458:             event.wait()\n459:             return foo + 1, event\n460: \n461:         x = torch.randn((1024, 1024), device=\"cuda\")\n462:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n463: \n464:         def run_iters(fn, compile=False):\n465:             if compile:\n466:                 fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n467:             for _ in range(10):\n468:                 with torch.cuda.stream(user_stream):\n469:                     torch.mm(x, x, out=foo)\n470:                     event.record()\n471:                 out = fn(foo)\n472:                 # let `fn` finish reading `foo` before writing to it in the next\n473:                 # iteration or `run_iters` call.\n474:                 torch.cuda.current_stream().synchronize()\n475:             return out\n476: \n477:         ref = run_iters(func, compile=False)\n478:         res = run_iters(func, compile=True)\n479:         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n480:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n481:         self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 3)\n482: \n483:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n484:     def test_cuda_event_method_create_stream_outside_of_compile(self):\n485:         def fn(x, cur_stream, new_stream):\n486:             x = torch.mul(x, 1)\n487:             x = torch.add(x, 2)\n488: \n489:             x = torch.add(x, 3)\n490: \n491:             event = cur_stream.record_event()\n492:             event.query()\n493: \n494:             new_stream.wait_event(event)\n495:             with torch.cuda.stream(new_stream):\n496:                 x = torch.add(x, 4)\n497: \n498:             new_event = torch.cuda.Event()\n499:             new_event.record(new_stream)\n500: \n501:             new_event.wait(cur_stream)\n502:             x = torch.add(x, 5)\n503: \n504:             # use new event to sync\n505:             new_event.synchronize()\n506: \n507:             x = torch.relu(x)\n508:             x = torch.cos(x)\n509:             return x\n510: \n511:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), device=\"cuda\")\n512:         cur_stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()\n513:         new_stream = torch.cuda.Stream()\n514:         ref = fn(x, cur_stream, new_stream)\n515:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n516:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\n517:         res = opt_fn(x, cur_stream, new_stream)\n518:         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n519:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n520:         self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 19)\n521: \n522:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n523:     def test_cuda_event_method(self):\n524:         def fn(x):\n525:             x = torch.mul(x, 1)\n526:             x = torch.add(x, 2)\n527: \n528:             cur_stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()\n529:             new_stream = torch.cuda.Stream()\n530: \n531:             x = torch.add(x, 3)\n532: \n533:             event = cur_stream.record_event()\n534:             event.query()\n535: \n536:             new_stream.wait_event(event)\n537:             with torch.cuda.stream(new_stream):\n538:                 x = torch.add(x, 4)\n539: \n540:             new_event = torch.cuda.Event()\n541:             new_event.record(new_stream)\n542: \n543:             new_event.wait(cur_stream)\n544:             x = torch.add(x, 5)\n545: \n546:             # use new event to sync\n547:             new_event.synchronize()\n548: \n549:             x = torch.relu(x)\n550:             x = torch.cos(x)\n551:             return x\n552: \n553:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), device=\"cuda\")\n554:         ref = fn(x)\n555:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n556:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\n557:         res = opt_fn(x)\n558:         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n559:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n560:         self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 19)\n561: \n562:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n563:     def test_cuda_device(self):\n564:         def fn(x):\n565:             with torch.cuda.device(x.device.index - 1):\n566:                 x = torch.sin(x + 1)\n567:             return x\n568: \n569:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), device=\"cuda\")\n570:         ref = fn(x)\n571:         opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(fn)\n572:         res = opt_fn(x)\n573:         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n574: \n575:     def test_autograd_profiler_enabled(self):\n576:         def fn(x):\n577:             if torch.autograd._profiler_enabled():\n578:                 return x + 1\n579:             else:\n580:                 return x - 1\n581: \n582:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), requires_grad=True)\n583:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n584:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n585: \n586:         if torch.autograd._profiler_enabled():\n587:             torch.autograd._disable_profiler()\n588:         assert not torch.autograd._profiler_enabled()\n589:         ref = fn(x)\n590:         res = opt_fn(x)\n591:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n592: \n593:         with torch.autograd.profiler.profile():\n594:             assert torch.autograd._profiler_enabled()\n595:             ref = fn(x)\n596:             res = opt_fn(x)\n597:             self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n598: \n599:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n600:     def test_autocast(self):\n601:         if not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():\n602:             raise unittest.SkipTest(\"requires bf16\")\n603: \n604:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n605:             def forward(self, x):\n606:                 a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n607:                 b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n608:                 d_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n609: \n610:                 with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cuda\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n611:                     e_float16 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n612:                     f_float16 = torch.mm(d_float32, e_float16)\n613:                 return f_float16\n614: \n615:         module = MyModule()\n616:         real = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n617:         real_device = real.device\n618:         real_dtype = real.dtype\n619: \n620:         graph, _ = torch._dynamo.export(module)(torch.tensor([[0.0, 0], [0, 0]]))\n621:         exported = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n622:         self.assertEqual(exported.device, real_device)\n623:         self.assertEqual(exported.dtype, real_dtype)\n624: \n625:         self.assertEqual(exported.device.type, \"cuda\")\n626:         self.assertEqual(exported.device.index, 0)\n627:         self.assertEqual(exported.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n628: \n629:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n630:     def test_cuda_amp_autocast(self):\n631:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n632:             def forward(self, x):\n633:                 a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n634:                 b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n635: \n636:                 with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cuda\", dtype=torch.float64):\n637:                     c_float64 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n638:                 return c_float64\n639: \n640:         module = MyModule()\n641:         real = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n642:         real_device = real.device\n643:         real_dtype = real.dtype\n644: \n645:         graph, _ = torch._dynamo.export(module)(torch.tensor([[0.0, 0], [0, 0]]))\n646:         exported = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n647:         self.assertEqual(exported.device, real_device)\n648:         self.assertEqual(exported.dtype, real_dtype)\n649: \n650:         self.assertEqual(exported.device.type, \"cuda\")\n651:         self.assertEqual(exported.device.index, 0)\n652:         self.assertEqual(exported.dtype, torch.float64)\n653: \n654:     def test_is_autocast_cpu_enabled(self):\n655:         def fn(a_float32, b_float32):\n656:             with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n657:                 c_float16 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n658:                 if torch.is_autocast_cpu_enabled():\n659:                     c_float16 = c_float16 + 1\n660:             return c_float16\n661: \n662:         a = torch.rand((8, 8))\n663:         b = torch.rand((8, 8))\n664:         ref = fn(a, b)\n665:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n666:         res = opt_fn(a, b)\n667:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n668: \n669:     @unittest.skipIf(\n670:         not PLATFORM_SUPPORTS_FLASH_ATTENTION,\n671:         \"Can't run fused SDPA on this platform\",\n672:     )\n673:     def test_autocast_sdpa(self):\n674:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n675:             def forward(self, query, key, value):\n676:                 with torch.autocast(\"cpu\"):\n677:                     with torch.autocast(\"cuda\", dtype=torch.float32):\n678:                         out = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(\n679:                             query, key, value, None, 0.0, True\n680:                         )\n681:                 return out\n682: \n683:         dtype = torch.float32\n684:         seq_len_q = 1\n685:         seq_len_k = 1\n686:         head_dim = 8\n687:         query = torch.ones(\n688:             1, 8, seq_len_q, head_dim, device=\"cuda\", dtype=dtype, requires_grad=True\n689:         )\n690:         key = torch.ones(\n691:             1, 8, seq_len_k, head_dim, device=\"cuda\", dtype=dtype, requires_grad=True\n692:         )\n693:         value = torch.ones(\n694:             1, 8, seq_len_k, head_dim, device=\"cuda\", dtype=dtype, requires_grad=True\n695:         )\n696: \n697:         module = MyModule()\n698:         real = module(query, key, value)\n699:         real_device = real.device\n700:         real_dtype = real.dtype\n701: \n702:         opt_mod = torch.compile(module, backend=\"inductor\")\n703:         compiled = opt_mod(query, key, value)\n704: \n705:         self.assertEqual(compiled.device, real_device)\n706:         self.assertEqual(compiled.dtype, real_dtype)\n707: \n708:         self.assertEqual(compiled.device.type, \"cuda\")\n709:         self.assertEqual(compiled.device.index, 0)\n710:         self.assertEqual(compiled.dtype, torch.float32)\n711: \n712:     def test_autocast_cpu(self):\n713:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n714:             def forward(self, x):\n715:                 a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n716:                 b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n717:                 d_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n718: \n719:                 with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n720:                     e_float16 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n721:                     f_float16 = torch.mm(d_float32, e_float16)\n722:                 return f_float16\n723: \n724:         module = MyModule()\n725:         real = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n726:         real_device = real.device\n727:         real_dtype = real.dtype\n728: \n729:         graph, _ = torch._dynamo.export(module)(torch.tensor([[0.0, 0], [0, 0]]))\n730:         exported = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n731:         self.assertEqual(exported.device, real_device)\n732:         self.assertEqual(exported.dtype, real_dtype)\n733: \n734:         self.assertEqual(exported.device.type, \"cpu\")\n735:         self.assertEqual(exported.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n736: \n737:     def test_autocast_cpu_graph_break(self):\n738:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n739:             def forward(self, x):\n740:                 a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n741:                 b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n742:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n743:                 d_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n744: \n745:                 with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n746:                     e_float16 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n747:                     torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n748:                     f_float16 = torch.mm(d_float32, e_float16)\n749:                 return f_float16\n750: \n751:         module = MyModule()\n752:         real = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n753:         real_device = real.device\n754:         real_dtype = real.dtype\n755: \n756:         opt = torch.compile(module, backend=\"eager\")\n757:         res = opt(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n758:         self.assertEqual(res.device, real_device)\n759:         self.assertEqual(res.dtype, real_dtype)\n760: \n761:         self.assertEqual(res.device.type, \"cpu\")\n762:         self.assertEqual(res.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n763: \n764:     def test_autocast_cpu_graph_break_2(self):\n765:         # Regression for: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/93890\n766:         def fn(x):\n767:             with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n768:                 x = torch.mm(x, x)\n769:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n770:                 x = torch.relu(x)\n771:             return x\n772: \n773:         x = torch.rand([4, 4])\n774:         self.assertEqual(x.dtype, torch.float32)\n775:         res = fn(x)\n776:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n777:         opt_res = opt_fn(x)\n778:         self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(res, opt_res))\n779:         self.assertEqual(res.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n780:         self.assertEqual(opt_res.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n781: \n782:     def test_autocast_cpu_graph_break_inner_fn(self):\n783:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n784:             @staticmethod\n785:             def mm_breaks(x, y):\n786:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n787:                 return torch.mm(x, y)\n788: \n789:             def forward(self, x):\n790:                 a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n791:                 b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n792: \n793:                 with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n794:                     torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n795:                     with torch.autocast(\n796:                         device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16, enabled=False\n797:                     ):\n798:                         torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n799:                         g_float32 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n800:                         with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n801:                             # Check that nested with non-inlineable function with graph break\n802:                             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n803:                             f_float16_1 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\n804:                     # We remember to exit the inner autocast correctly to outer\n805:                     # even after graph breaks\n806:                     f_float16 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\n807:                     assert f_float16.dtype == f_float16_1.dtype\n808:                 return f_float16, g_float32\n809: \n810:         module = MyModule()\n811:         real_16, real_32 = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n812:         real_device_16 = real_16.device\n813:         real_dtype_16 = real_16.dtype\n814:         real_device_32 = real_32.device\n815:         real_dtype_32 = real_32.dtype\n816: \n817:         graph = torch.compile(module, backend=\"eager\")\n818:         out_16, out_32 = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n819:         self.assertEqual(out_16.device, real_device_16)\n820:         self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, real_dtype_16)\n821:         self.assertEqual(out_32.device, real_device_32)\n822:         self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, real_dtype_32)\n823: \n824:         self.assertEqual(out_16.device.type, \"cpu\")\n825:         self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n826:         self.assertEqual(out_32.device.type, \"cpu\")\n827:         self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, torch.float32)\n828: \n829:     def test_autocast_graph_break_method(self):\n830:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n831:             def __init__(self, bias):\n832:                 super().__init__()\n833:                 self.bias = bias\n834: \n835:             def mm_not_break(self, x, y):\n836:                 return torch.mm(x, y) + self.bias\n837: \n838:             def mm_breaks(self, x, y):\n839:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n840:                 return torch.mm(x, y) + self.bias\n841: \n842:             def forward(self, x):\n843:                 a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n844:                 b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n845: \n846:                 with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n847:                     with torch.autocast(\n848:                         device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16, enabled=False\n849:                     ):\n850:                         g_float32 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n851:                     f_float16 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\n852: \n853:                     assert (\n854:                         f_float16[0][0] == self.mm_not_break(a_float32, b_float32)[0][0]\n855:                     )\n856:                 return f_float16, g_float32\n857: \n858:         module = MyModule(bias=torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16))\n859: \n860:         with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n861:             # Autocast doesn't work on addition, so we need the bias to be `bfloat16`\n862:             res = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.float32) + torch.rand(\n863:                 (8, 8), device=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16\n864:             )\n865:             self.assertEqual(res.dtype, torch.float32)\n866: \n867:         real_16, real_32 = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n868:         real_device_16 = real_16.device\n869:         real_dtype_16 = real_16.dtype\n870:         real_device_32 = real_32.device\n871:         real_dtype_32 = real_32.dtype\n872: \n873:         graph = torch.compile(module, backend=\"eager\")\n874:         out_16, out_32 = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n875:         self.assertEqual(out_16.device, real_device_16)\n876:         self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, real_dtype_16)\n877:         self.assertEqual(out_32.device, real_device_32)\n878:         self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, real_dtype_32)\n879: \n880:         self.assertEqual(out_16.device.type, \"cpu\")\n881:         self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n882:         self.assertEqual(out_32.device.type, \"cpu\")\n883:         self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, torch.float32)\n884: \n885:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n886:     def test_autocast_float64(self):\n887:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n888:             def forward(self, x):\n889:                 a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n890:                 b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n891:                 d_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n892: \n893:                 with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cuda\", dtype=torch.float64):\n894:                     e_float64 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n895:                     f_float64 = torch.mm(d_float32, e_float64)\n896:                 return f_float64\n897: \n898:         module = MyModule()\n899:         real = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n900:         real_device = real.device\n901:         real_dtype = real.dtype\n902: \n903:         graph, _ = torch._dynamo.export(module)(torch.tensor([[0.0, 0], [0, 0]]))\n904:         exported = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n905:         self.assertEqual(exported.device, real_device)\n906:         self.assertEqual(exported.dtype, real_dtype)\n907: \n908:         self.assertEqual(exported.device.index, 0)\n909:         self.assertEqual(exported.dtype, torch.float64)\n910: \n911:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n912:     def test_autocast_device(self):\n913:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n914:             def forward(self, x):\n915:                 a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n916:                 b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n917:                 d_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n918: \n919:                 with torch.autocast(\"cuda\"):\n920:                     e_float64 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n921:                     f_float64 = torch.mm(d_float32, e_float64)\n922:                 return f_float64\n923: \n924:         module = MyModule()\n925:         real = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n926:         real_device = real.device\n927:         real_dtype = real.dtype\n928: \n929:         graph, _ = torch._dynamo.export(module)(torch.tensor([[0.0, 0], [0, 0]]))\n930:         exported = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n931:         self.assertEqual(exported.device, real_device)\n932:         self.assertEqual(exported.dtype, real_dtype)\n933: \n934:         self.assertEqual(exported.device.index, 0)\n935:         self.assertEqual(exported.dtype, torch.float16)\n936: \n937:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n938:     def test_autocast_arguments_binding(self):\n939:         def f1(x):\n940:             with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cuda\", enabled=False):\n941:                 x = torch.sin(x + 1)\n942:             return x\n943: \n944:         def f2(x):\n945:             with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", enabled=False):\n946:                 x = torch.cos(x + 1)\n947:             return x\n948: \n949:         x = torch.rand([2, 3])\n950:         ref1 = f1(x)\n951:         ref2 = f2(x)\n952:         opt_f1 = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")(f1)\n953:         opt_f2 = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")(f2)\n954:         res1 = opt_f1(x)\n955:         res2 = opt_f2(x)\n956:         self.assertTrue(same(ref1, res1))\n957:         self.assertTrue(same(ref2, res2))\n958: \n959:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n960:     def test_autocast_decorator(self):\n961:         def autocast_func(orig_func):\n962:             @torch.amp.autocast(device_type=\"cuda\", dtype=torch.float16)\n963:             def new_fwd(*args, **kwargs):\n964:                 return orig_func(*args, **kwargs)\n965: \n966:             return new_fwd\n967: \n968:         def autocast_func_cuda(orig_func):\n969:             @torch.autocast(device_type=\"cuda\", dtype=torch.float16)\n970:             def new_fwd(*args, **kwargs):\n971:                 return orig_func(*args, **kwargs)\n972: \n973:             return new_fwd\n974: \n975:         def autocast_func_cpu(orig_func):\n976:             @torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.float16)\n977:             def new_fwd(*args, **kwargs):\n978:                 return orig_func(*args, **kwargs)\n979: \n980:             return new_fwd\n981: \n982:         def mm(a, b):\n983:             return torch.mm(a, b)\n984: \n985:         mm_float16 = autocast_func(mm)\n986:         mm_float16_cuda = autocast_func_cuda(mm)\n987:         mm_float16_cpu = autocast_func_cpu(mm)\n988: \n989:         def fn(a, b):\n990:             return mm_float16(a, b), mm_float16_cuda(a, b), mm_float16_cpu(a, b)\n991: \n992:         a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n993:         b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n994: \n995:         ref = fn(a_float32, b_float32)\n996:         opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(fn)\n997:         res = opt_fn(a_float32, b_float32)\n998:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n999:         self.assertTrue(res[0].dtype == torch.float16)\n1000:         self.assertTrue(res[1].dtype == torch.float16)\n1001: \n1002:     @parametrize(\n1003:         \"Ctx\",\n1004:         [CustomizedCtxManagerWithGraphBreak, customized_ctx_manager_with_graph_break],\n1005:         name_fn=lambda x: x.__name__,\n1006:     )\n1007:     def test_generic_ctx_manager_with_graph_break(self, Ctx):\n1008:         def fn(x):\n1009:             with Ctx(False):\n1010:                 # body runs on eager\n1011:                 if torch.is_grad_enabled():\n1012:                     z = x + 1000\n1013:                 else:\n1014:                     y = x * 2\n1015:                     z = y.sin() + 3\n1016:             return z\n1017: \n1018:         self.assertTrue(torch.is_grad_enabled())\n1019:         x = torch.randn(2, 3, requires_grad=True)\n1020:         expected = fn(x)\n1021:         got = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n1022:         self.assertEqual(expected, got)\n1023:         self.assertTrue(torch.is_grad_enabled())\n1024:         self.assertFalse(got.requires_grad)  # since it was run under torch.no_grad.\n1025: \n1026:     def test_return_context_manager(self):\n1027:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1028:         def f(x):\n1029:             cm = CustomizedCtxManager(False)\n1030:             with cm:\n1031:                 pass\n1032:             return cm\n1033: \n1034:         x = torch.randn(2, 3)\n1035:         cm = f(x)\n1036:         self.assertFalse(cm.mode)\n1037: \n1038:     def test_return_context_manager_with_graph_break(self):\n1039:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)\n1040:         def f(x):\n1041:             cm = CustomizedCtxManager(False)\n1042:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1043:             with cm:\n1044:                 pass\n1045:             return cm\n1046: \n1047:         x = torch.randn(2, 3)\n1048:         cm = f(x)\n1049:         self.assertFalse(cm.mode)\n1050: \n1051:     @torch._dynamo.config.patch(enable_trace_contextlib=True)\n1052:     @parametrize(\n1053:         \"Ctx\",\n1054:         [CustomizedCtxManager, customized_ctx_manager],\n1055:         name_fn=lambda x: x.__name__,\n1056:     )\n1057:     def test_generic_context_manager(self, Ctx):\n1058:         def fn(x):\n1059:             with Ctx(True):\n1060:                 x = x + 1\n1061:                 if torch.is_grad_enabled():\n1062:                     x = x * 2\n1063:                 x = torch.relu(x)\n1064:             return x - 1\n1065: \n1066:         x = torch.rand(2, 3)\n1067:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1068:         opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)(fn)\n1069: \n1070:         with torch.no_grad():\n1071:             ref = fn(x)\n1072:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1073:             self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1074:             self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n1075:             self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 6)\n1076: \n1077:         with torch.enable_grad():\n1078:             ref = fn(x)\n1079:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1080:             self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1081:             self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1082:             self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 12)\n1083: \n1084:     @torch._dynamo.config.patch(enable_trace_contextlib=True)\n1085:     @parametrize(\n1086:         \"Ctx\",\n1087:         [CustomizedCtxManager, customized_ctx_manager],\n1088:         name_fn=lambda x: x.__name__,\n1089:     )\n1090:     def test_nested_generic_context_manager(self, Ctx):\n1091:         def fn(x):\n1092:             with Ctx(True):\n1093:                 x = x + 1\n1094:                 if torch.is_grad_enabled():\n1095:                     x = x * 2\n1096:                 with Ctx(False):\n1097:                     if torch.is_grad_enabled():\n1098:                         x = x - 3\n1099:                     x = x * 1.5\n1100:                 x = torch.relu(x)\n1101:             return x - 1\n1102: \n1103:         x = torch.rand(2, 3)\n1104:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1105:         opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)(fn)\n1106: \n1107:         with torch.no_grad():\n1108:             ref = fn(x)\n1109:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1110:             self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1111:             self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n1112:             self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 9)\n1113: \n1114:         with torch.enable_grad():\n1115:             ref = fn(x)\n1116:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1117:             self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1118:             self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1119:             self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 18)\n1120: \n1121:     @torch._dynamo.config.patch(enable_trace_contextlib=True)\n1122:     @parametrize(\n1123:         \"Ctx\",\n1124:         [CustomizedCtxManager, customized_ctx_manager],\n1125:         name_fn=lambda x: x.__name__,\n1126:     )\n1127:     def test_generic_context_manager_with_graph_break(self, Ctx):\n1128:         def fn(x):\n1129:             with Ctx(True):\n1130:                 x = x + 1\n1131:                 if torch.is_grad_enabled():\n1132:                     x = x * 2\n1133:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1134:                 x = torch.relu(x)\n1135:             return x - 1\n1136: \n1137:         x = torch.rand(2, 3)\n1138:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1139:         opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=cnts, fullgraph=False)(fn)\n1140: \n1141:         with torch.no_grad():\n1142:             ref = fn(x)\n1143:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1144:             self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1145:             if Ctx is CustomizedCtxManager:\n1146:                 self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1147:                 self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 2)\n1148: \n1149:         with torch.enable_grad():\n1150:             ref = fn(x)\n1151:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1152:             self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1153:             if Ctx is CustomizedCtxManager:\n1154:                 self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 4)\n1155:                 self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 4)\n1156: \n1157:     @torch._dynamo.config.patch(enable_trace_contextlib=True)\n1158:     @parametrize(\n1159:         \"Ctx\",\n1160:         [CustomizedCtxManager, customized_ctx_manager],\n1161:         name_fn=lambda x: x.__name__,\n1162:     )\n1163:     def test_nested_generic_context_manager_with_graph_break(self, Ctx):\n1164:         def fn(x):\n1165:             with Ctx(True):\n1166:                 x = x + 1\n1167:                 if torch.is_grad_enabled():\n1168:                     x = x * 2\n1169:                 with Ctx(False):\n1170:                     if torch.is_grad_enabled():\n1171:                         x = x - 3\n1172:                     torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1173:                     x = x * 1.5\n1174:                 x = torch.relu(x)\n1175:             return x - 1\n1176: \n1177:         x = torch.rand(2, 3)\n1178:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1179:         opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=cnts, fullgraph=False)(fn)\n1180: \n1181:         with torch.no_grad():\n1182:             ref = fn(x)\n1183:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1184:             self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1185:             if Ctx is CustomizedCtxManager:\n1186:                 self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 4)\n1187:                 self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 4)\n1188: \n1189:         torch._dynamo.reset()\n1190:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1191:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts, fullgraph=False)\n1192: \n1193:         with torch.enable_grad():\n1194:             ref = fn(x)\n1195:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1196:             self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1197:             if Ctx is CustomizedCtxManager:\n1198:                 self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 4)\n1199:                 self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 4)\n1200: \n1201:     def test_graph_break_inlining_grad(self):\n1202:         def gn(z):\n1203:             with torch.no_grad():\n1204:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1205:                 return torch.sin(z)\n1206: \n1207:         def fn(x, y, z):\n1208:             a = torch.mm(x, y)\n1209:             z = gn(z)\n1210:             return a\n1211: \n1212:         torch._dynamo.reset()\n1213:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1214:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts, fullgraph=False)\n1215:         x = torch.randn(4, 4, requires_grad=True)\n1216:         y = torch.randn(4, 4, requires_grad=True)\n1217:         z = torch.randn(4)\n1218:         opt_fn(x, y, z).sum().backward()\n1219: \n1220:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1221: \n1222:     def _graph_break_inlining_autocast_test_helper(self, device):\n1223:         def gn(x, y):\n1224:             with torch.autocast(device_type=device, dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n1225:                 z = torch.mm(x, y)\n1226:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1227:                 return torch.sin(z)\n1228: \n1229:         def fn(x, y):\n1230:             z = torch.mm(x, y)\n1231:             z = z + gn(x, y)\n1232:             return z\n1233: \n1234:         x = torch.rand(3, 3).to(device)\n1235:         y = torch.rand(3, 3).to(device)\n1236:         opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")(fn)\n1237:         ref = fn(x, y)\n1238:         res = opt_fn(x, y)\n1239:         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n1240: \n1241:     def test_graph_break_inlining_autocast(self):\n1242:         for device in [\"cuda\", \"cpu\"]:\n1243:             if device == \"cuda\" and not (\n1244:                 torch.cuda.is_available() and torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()\n1245:             ):\n1246:                 continue\n1247:             self._graph_break_inlining_autocast_test_helper(device)\n1248: \n1249:     def test_disable_saved_tensors_hooks(self):\n1250:         def fn(z):\n1251:             @torch.autograd.graph.disable_saved_tensors_hooks(\"This is not supported\")\n1252:             def f(x, y):\n1253:                 return x + y\n1254: \n1255:             x, y = (\n1256:                 torch.ones(\n1257:                     1,\n1258:                 ),\n1259:                 torch.zeros(\n1260:                     1,\n1261:                 ),\n1262:             )\n1263:             return f(x, y)\n1264: \n1265:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n1266:         torch.compile(fn, backend=eager, fullgraph=True)(torch.randn(()))\n1267: \n1268:         graph = eager.graphs[0]\n1269:         actual = normalize_gm(graph.print_readable(False))\n1270: \n1271: EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n1397:         torch.compile(fn, backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(torch.randn(()))\n1398: \n1399:         def check_graph(actual, expected):  # noqa: F841\n1400: self.assertExpectedInline(\n1405:             actual,\n1406:             \"\"\"\\\n1407: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n1408:     def forward(self, L_x_: \"f32[]\"):\n1409:         l_x_ = L_x_\n1410: \n1411:         _saved_tensors_hooks_disable = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_disable('This is not supported');  _saved_tensors_hooks_disable = None\n1412: \n1413:         y: \"f32[]\" = l_x_ + 1;  l_x_ = None\n1414: \n1415:         _saved_tensors_hooks_enable = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_enable();  _saved_tensors_hooks_enable = None\n1416:         return (y,)\n1417: \"\"\",  # NOQA: B950\n1418:         )\n1419: \n1420:         graph = eager.graphs[1]\n1421:         actual = normalize_gm(graph.print_readable(False))\n1422:         self.assertExpectedInline(\n1423:             actual,\n1424:             \"\"\"\\\n1425: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n1426:     def forward(self, L_y_: \"f32[]\"):\n1427:         l_y_ = L_y_\n1428: \n1429:         _saved_tensors_hooks_disable = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_disable('This is not supported');  _saved_tensors_hooks_disable = None\n1430: \n1431:         mul: \"f32[]\" = l_y_ * 2;  l_y_ = None\n1432: \n1433:         _saved_tensors_hooks_enable = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_enable();  _saved_tensors_hooks_enable = None\n1434:         return (mul,)\n1435: \"\"\",  # NOQA: B950\n1436:         )\n1437: \n1438:     def test_context_wrapping_grad_mode_decorator(self):\n1439:         ctx_wrappers = [(torch.enable_grad, True), (torch.no_grad, False)]\n1440:         for call in [True, False]:\n1441:             for i in range(2):\n1442:                 torch._dynamo.reset()\n1443: \n1444:                 ctx_wrapper, _ = ctx_wrappers[i]\n1445:                 ctx_wrapper_inverse, mode_inverse = ctx_wrappers[(i + 1) % 2]\n1446: \n1447:                 def fn(x):\n1448:                     def inner_func(x):\n1449:                         return x.sin()\n1450: \n1451:                     with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n1452:                         if call:\n1453:                             inner_func = ctx_wrapper()(inner_func)\n1454:                         else:\n1455:                             inner_func = ctx_wrapper(inner_func)\n1456: \n1457:                         # Calling no_grad or enabled_grad should not mutate global state\n1458:                         assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n1459: \n1460:                     with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n1461:                         return inner_func(x)\n1462: \n1463:                 x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n1464:                 opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1465:                 self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1466:                 self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1467: \n1468:     def test_context_wrapping_grad_mode_nested_function_decorator(self):\n1469:         ctx_wrappers = [(torch.enable_grad, True), (torch.no_grad, False)]\n1470: \n1471:         for call in [True, False]:\n1472:             for i in range(2):\n1473:                 torch._dynamo.reset()\n1474: \n1475:                 ctx_wrapper, _ = ctx_wrappers[i]\n1476:                 ctx_wrapper_inverse, mode_inverse = ctx_wrappers[(i + 1) % 2]\n1477: \n1478:                 def fn(x):\n1479:                     with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n1480:                         if call:\n1481: \n1482:                             @ctx_wrapper()\n1483:                             def inner_func(x):\n1484:                                 return x.sin()\n1485: \n1486:                         else:\n1487: \n1488:                             @ctx_wrapper\n1489:                             def inner_func(x):\n1490:                                 return x.sin()\n1491: \n1492:                         # Calling no_grad or enabled_grad should not mutate global state\n1493:                         assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n1494: \n1495:                     with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n1496:                         return inner_func(x)\n1497: \n1498:                 x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n1499:                 opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1500:                 self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1501:                 self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1502: \n1503:     def test_context_wrapping_set_grad_enabled_nested_function(self):\n1504:         modes = [True, False]\n1505:         for decorator in [True, False]:\n1506:             for i in range(2):\n1507:                 torch._dynamo.reset()\n1508: \n1509:                 mode = modes[i]\n1510:                 mode_inverse = modes[(i + 1) % 2]\n1511: \n1512:                 def fn(x):\n1513:                     with torch.set_grad_enabled(mode_inverse):\n1514:                         if decorator:\n1515: \n1516:                             @torch.set_grad_enabled(mode)\n1517:                             def inner_func(x):\n1518:                                 return x.sin()\n1519: \n1520:                         else:\n1521: \n1522:                             def inner_func(x):\n1523:                                 return x.sin()\n1524: \n1525:                             inner_func = torch.set_grad_enabled(mode)(inner_func)\n1526: \n1527:                         # Consuming set_grad_enabled by calling it on a function\n1528:                         # should not mutate global state\n1529:                         assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n1530: \n1531:                     with torch.set_grad_enabled(mode_inverse):\n1532:                         return inner_func(x)\n1533: \n1534:             x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n1535:             opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1536:             self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1537:             self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1538: \n1539:     def test_inactive_context_graph_break_local(self):\n1540:         def fn(x):\n1541:             x = x + 1\n1542:             ctx = torch.set_grad_enabled(True)\n1543:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1544:             with ctx:\n1545:                 x = x + 1\n1546:             return x\n1547: \n1548:         x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=False)\n1549:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1550:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n1551:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1552:         self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1553:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1554: \n1555:     def test_inactive_context_graph_break_local_nullctx(self):\n1556:         import contextlib\n1557: \n1558:         # test with context manager that results in None target_values\n1559:         def fn(x):\n1560:             x = x + 1\n1561:             ctx = contextlib.nullcontext()\n1562:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1563:             with ctx:\n1564:                 x = x + 1\n1565:             return x\n1566: \n1567:         x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=False)\n1568:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1569:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n1570:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1571:         self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1572:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1573: \n1574:     def test_inactive_context_graph_break_local_nullctx2(self):\n1575:         import contextlib\n1576: \n1577:         # test with nullcontext where graph break happens\n1578:         # in an inlined function that returns something\n1579:         def gn():\n1580:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1581:             return [0, 1, 2]\n1582: \n1583:         def fn(x):\n1584:             x = x + 1\n1585:             ctx = contextlib.nullcontext()\n1586:             lst = gn()\n1587:             with ctx:\n1588:                 x = x + lst[1]\n1589:             return x\n1590: \n1591:         x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=False)\n1592:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1593:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n1594:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1595:         self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1596:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1597: \n1598:     def test_inactive_context_graph_break_stack(self):\n1599:         def gn(ctx):\n1600:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1601:             return ctx\n1602: \n1603:         def fn(x):\n1604:             x = x + 1\n1605:             ctx = gn(torch.set_grad_enabled(True))\n1606:             # we expect a graph break on next line as well\n1607:             with ctx:\n1608:                 x = x + 1\n1609:             return x\n1610: \n1611:         x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=False)\n1612:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1613:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n1614:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1615:         self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1616: \n1617:     def test_inactive_context_graph_break_stack2(self):\n1618:         def gn(x, ctx, y, z, dummy):\n1619:             with ctx:\n1620:                 return x * y * z\n1621: \n1622:         def fn(x):\n1623:             x = x + 1\n1624:             x = gn(x, torch.set_grad_enabled(True), 2, 3, torch._dynamo.graph_break())\n1625:             return x\n1626: \n1627:         x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=False)\n1628:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1629:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n1630:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1631:         self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1632:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1633: \n1634:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager1(self):\n1635:         modified_backend_state = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1636: \n1637:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1638:         def check_backend_state_is_modified():\n1639:             self.assertEqual(\n1640:                 torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends(), modified_backend_state\n1641:             )\n1642: \n1643:         def f(x):\n1644:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(\n1645:                 # pyre-fixme[16]: Module `torch.nn.attention` has no attribute `SDPBackend`.\n1646:                 [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1647:             ):\n1648:                 output = torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x).to(\n1649:                     torch.float32\n1650:                 )\n1651:                 check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1652: \n1653:             return output\n1654: \n1655:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1656:         opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2, 2, 2).to(dtype=torch.float16))\n1657: \n1658:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager2(self):\n1659:         original_backend_state = set(torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends())\n1660:         modified_backend_state = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1661: \n1662:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1663:         def check_backend_state_is_original():\n1664:             self.assertEqual(\n1665:                 set(torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends()),\n1666:                 original_backend_state,\n1667:             )\n1668: \n1669:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1670:         def check_backend_state_is_modified():\n1671:             self.assertEqual(\n1672:                 torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends(), modified_backend_state\n1673:             )\n1674: \n1675:         def g(x):\n1676:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1677:             output = torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x).to(\n1678:                 torch.float32\n1679:             )\n1680:             check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1681:             return output\n1682: \n1683:         def f(x):\n1684:             check_backend_state_is_original()\n1685:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(\n1686:                 # pyre-fixme[16]: Module `torch.nn.attention` has no attribute `SDPBackend`.\n1687:                 [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1688:             ):\n1689:                 output1 = torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x).to(\n1690:                     torch.float32\n1691:                 )\n1692:                 check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1693: \n1694:                 # graph break\n1695:                 output2 = g(x)\n1696: \n1697:                 output3 = torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x).to(\n1698:                     torch.float32\n1699:                 )\n1700:                 check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1701: \n1702:             check_backend_state_is_original()\n1703: \n1704:             return output1 + output2 + output3\n1705: \n1706:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1707:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=cnts)\n1708:         opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2, 2, 2).to(dtype=torch.float16))\n1709:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 3)\n1710: \n1711:     # test sdpa_kernel graph break with 2 arguments\n1712:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager3(self):\n1713:         modified_backend_state = {\n1714:             torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH,\n1715:             torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION,\n1716:         }\n1717: \n1718:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1719:         def check_backend_state_is_modified():\n1720:             self.assertEqual(\n1721:                 set(torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends()),\n1722:                 modified_backend_state,\n1723:             )\n1724: \n1725:         def f(x):\n1726:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(\n1727:                 # pyre-fixme[16]: Module `torch.nn.attention` has no attribute `SDPBackend`.\n1728:                 [\n1729:                     torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH,\n1730:                     torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION,\n1731:                 ]\n1732:             ):\n1733:                 # FLASH_ATTENTION may not be supported, but we're not actually\n1734:                 # doing any sdpa\n1735:                 x = x + 1\n1736:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1737:                 check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1738:                 x = x + 1\n1739: \n1740:             return x\n1741: \n1742:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\")\n1743:         opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2))\n1744: \n1745:     # Regression test to make sure dynamo won't crash on these kwargs.\n1746:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager_kwargs(self):\n1747:         backends = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1748: \n1749:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1750:         def check_backend_state_is_modified():\n1751:             self.assertEqual(\n1752:                 set(torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends()),\n1753:                 set(backends),\n1754:             )\n1755: \n1756:         def f(x):\n1757:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, set_priority=True):\n1758:                 x = x + 1\n1759:                 check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1760:                 x = x + 1\n1761: \n1762:             return x\n1763: \n1764:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\")\n1765:         opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2))\n1766: \n1767:     # Regression test to make sure dynamo won't graph break on calling functions\n1768:     # decorated with special context manager.\n1769:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager_as_decorator(self):\n1770:         SDPA_BACKEND_PRIORITY = [\n1771:             torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH,\n1772:             torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.EFFICIENT_ATTENTION,\n1773:             torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION,\n1774:         ]\n1775: \n1776:         @torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(\n1777:             backends=SDPA_BACKEND_PRIORITY, set_priority=True\n1778:         )\n1779:         def scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, *args, **kwargs):\n1780:             return torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(\n1781:                 q, k, v, *args, **kwargs\n1782:             )\n1783: \n1784:         def f(x):\n1785:             return scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x)\n1786: \n1787:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1788:         x = torch.rand(16, 16, 64, 256, dtype=torch.float16)\n1789:         ref = f(x)\n1790:         res = opt_f(x)\n1791: \n1792:         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n1793: \n1794:     # Regression test to make sure the value of set_priority is used correctly.\n1795:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager_set_priority(self):\n1796:         backends = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1797:         default_priority = torch._C._get_sdp_priority_order()\n1798: \n1799:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1800:         def check_backend_priority(changed: bool):\n1801:             self.assertEqual(\n1802:                 changed,\n1803:                 torch._C._get_sdp_priority_order() != default_priority,\n1804:             )\n1805: \n1806:         def f(x):\n1807:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, set_priority=True):\n1808:                 x = x + 1\n1809:                 check_backend_priority(changed=True)\n1810:                 x = x + 1\n1811: \n1812:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, set_priority=False):\n1813:                 x = x + 1\n1814:                 check_backend_priority(changed=False)\n1815:                 x = x + 1\n1816: \n1817:             return x\n1818: \n1819:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\")\n1820:         opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2))\n1821: \n1822:     def test_torch_profiler_use_after_with_block(self):\n1823:         counters.clear()\n1824: \n1825:         def fn(x):\n1826:             with torch.profiler.profile() as p:\n1827:                 pass\n1828:             p.profiler.kineto_results.experimental_event_tree()\n1829:             return x + 1\n1830: \n1831:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n1832:         x = torch.ones(1)\n1833:         ref = fn(x)\n1834:         res = opt_fn(x)\n1835:         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n1836:         self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 1)\n1837: \n1838: \n1839: class ContextlibContextManagerTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\n1840:     def setUp(self):\n1841:         self._prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib\n1842:         self._u_prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest\n1843:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = True\n1844:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = True\n1845: \n1846:     def tearDown(self):\n1847:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = self._prev\n1848:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = self._u_prev\n1849: \n1850:     def test_ctx_basic0(self):\n1851:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n1852:         def set_default_dtype(dtype):\n1853:             old_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()\n1854:             try:\n1855:                 torch.set_default_dtype(dtype)\n1856:                 yield\n1857:             finally:\n1858:                 torch.set_default_dtype(old_dtype)\n1859: \n1860:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n1861: \n1862:         @torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=True)\n1863:         def fn():\n1864:             with set_default_dtype(torch.float64):\n1865:                 x = torch.tensor([3.0, 3.0 + 5.0j])\n1866:             return x\n1867: \n1868:         y = fn()\n1869:         self.assertEqual(y.dtype, torch.complex128)\n1870:         graph = eager.graphs[0]\n1871:         actual = normalize_gm(graph.print_readable(False))\n1872: \n1873:         self.assertExpectedInline(\n1874:             actual,\n1875:             \"\"\"\\\n1876: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n1877:     def forward(self):\n1878:         set_default_dtype = torch.set_default_dtype(torch.float64);  set_default_dtype = None\n1879: \n1880:         x: \"c128[2]\" = torch.tensor([3.0, (3+5j)])\n1881: \n1882:         set_default_dtype_1 = torch.set_default_dtype(torch.float32);  set_default_dtype_1 = None\n1883:         return (x,)\n1884: \"\"\",\n1885:         )\n1886: \n1887:     def test_ctx_basic1(self):\n1888:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n1889:         def compute_sin(x):\n1890:             try:\n1891:                 yield x.sin()\n1892:             finally:\n1893:                 pass\n1894: \n1895:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1896:         def fn(x):\n1897:             with compute_sin(x) as y:\n1898:                 return y.cos()\n1899: \n1900:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n1901:         y = fn(x)\n1902:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n1903: \n1904:     def test_change_parent_nonlocal_0(self):\n1905:         # test if a nonlocal actually gets propagated\n1906:         z = 0\n1907:         k = 0\n1908: \n1909:         def create_ctx():\n1910:             @contextmanager\n1911:             def ctx(x):\n1912:                 nonlocal z\n1913:                 nonlocal k\n1914:                 try:\n1915:                     k = 100\n1916:                     yield x.sin()\n1917:                 finally:\n1918:                     pass\n1919: \n1920:             return ctx\n1921: \n1922:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n1923:             nonlocal z\n1924:             with ctx(x) as y:\n1925:                 z = k\n1926:                 return y.cos()\n1927: \n1928:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1929:         def fn(x):\n1930:             ctx = create_ctx()\n1931:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n1932: \n1933:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n1934:         y = fn(x)\n1935:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n1936:         self.assertEqual(z, 100)\n1937:         self.assertEqual(k, 100)\n1938: \n1939:     def test_change_parent_nonlocal_1(self):\n1940:         # test if finally is executed and it is reading the correct variable\n1941:         z = 1\n1942:         k = 2\n1943: \n1944:         def create_ctx():\n1945:             @contextmanager\n1946:             def ctx(x):\n1947:                 nonlocal z\n1948:                 nonlocal k\n1949:                 try:\n1950:                     yield x.sin()\n1951:                 finally:\n1952:                     k = z\n1953: \n1954:             return ctx\n1955: \n1956:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n1957:             nonlocal z\n1958:             z = 100\n1959:             with ctx(x) as y:\n1960:                 return y.cos()\n1961: \n1962:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1963:         def fn(x):\n1964:             ctx = create_ctx()\n1965:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n1966: \n1967:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n1968:         y = fn(x)\n1969:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n1970:         self.assertEqual(z, 100)\n1971:         self.assertEqual(k, 100)\n1972: \n1973:     def test_globals_change_in_other_file(self):\n1974:         @contextmanager\n1975:         def update_global_ctx():\n1976:             global _variable, _variable1\n1977:             try:\n1978:                 _variable += 1\n1979:                 _variable1 += 1\n1980:                 yield\n1981:             finally:\n1982:                 pass\n1983: \n1984:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1985:         def fn(x):\n1986:             with update_global_ctx():\n1987:                 pass\n1988: \n1989:             with test_functions.update_global_ctx(x) as a:\n1990:                 # Ensure that the updated global values are read\n1991:                 test_functions.constant3(2, 3)\n1992:                 return x * a * (_variable + _variable1 + test_functions._variable)\n1993: \n1994:         res = fn(torch.ones(10))\n1995:         self.assertEqual(_variable, 1)\n1996:         self.assertEqual(_variable1, 1)\n1997:         # Ensure that the reconstructed bytecode updates the global value in the\n1998:         # other file.\n1999:         self.assertEqual(test_functions._variable, 1)\n2000:         self.assertEqual(res, 3 * torch.ones(10))\n2001: \n2002:     def test_change_parent_global_0(self):\n2003:         # test if a global actually gets propagated\n2004:         global z_glb, k_glb\n2005:         z_glb, k_glb = 0, 0\n2006: \n2007:         def create_ctx():\n2008:             @contextmanager\n2009:             def ctx(x):\n2010:                 global k_glb\n2011:                 try:\n2012:                     k_glb = 100\n2013:                     yield x.sin()\n2014:                 finally:\n2015:                     pass\n2016: \n2017:             return ctx\n2018: \n2019:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n2020:             global z_glb\n2021:             with ctx(x) as y:\n2022:                 z_glb = k_glb\n2023:                 return y.cos()\n2024: \n2025:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n2026:         def fn(x):\n2027:             ctx = create_ctx()\n2028:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n2029: \n2030:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2031:         y = fn(x)\n2032:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n2033:         self.assertEqual(z_glb, 100)\n2034:         self.assertEqual(k_glb, 100)\n2035: \n2036:     def test_change_parent_global_1(self):\n2037:         # test if finally is executed and it is reading the correct variable\n2038:         global z_glb, k_glb\n2039:         z_glb, k_glb = 0, 0\n2040: \n2041:         def create_ctx():\n2042:             @contextmanager\n2043:             def ctx(x):\n2044:                 global z_glb, k_glb\n2045:                 try:\n2046:                     yield x.sin()\n2047:                 finally:\n2048:                     k_glb = z_glb\n2049: \n2050:             return ctx\n2051: \n2052:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n2053:             global z_glb\n2054:             z_glb = 100\n2055:             with ctx(x) as y:\n2056:                 return y.cos()\n2057: \n2058:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n2059:         def fn(x):\n2060:             ctx = create_ctx()\n2061:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n2062: \n2063:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2064:         y = fn(x)\n2065:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n2066:         self.assertEqual(z_glb, 100)\n2067:         self.assertEqual(k_glb, 100)\n2068: \n2069:     def test_change_parent_0(self):\n2070:         def create_ctx():\n2071:             @contextlib.contextmanager\n2072:             def ctx(x):\n2073:                 try:\n2074:                     yield x.sin()\n2075:                 finally:\n2076:                     pass\n2077: \n2078:             return ctx\n2079: \n2080:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n2081:             with ctx(x) as y:\n2082:                 return y.cos()\n2083: \n2084:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n2085:         def fn(x):\n2086:             ctx = create_ctx()\n2087:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n2088: \n2089:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2090:         y = fn(x)\n2091:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n2092: \n2093:     def test_change_parent_1(self):\n2094:         def create_ctx(x):\n2095:             @contextlib.contextmanager\n2096:             def ctx():\n2097:                 try:\n2098:                     yield x.sin()\n2099:                 finally:\n2100:                     pass\n2101: \n2102:             return ctx\n2103: \n2104:         def run_ctx(ctx):\n2105:             with ctx() as y:\n2106:                 return y.cos()\n2107: \n2108:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n2109:         def fn(x):\n2110:             ctx = create_ctx(x)\n2111:             return run_ctx(ctx)\n2112: \n2113:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2114:         y = fn(x)\n2115:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n2116: \n2117:     def test_graph_break_inside_ctx(self):\n2118:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2119:         def whoo(x):\n2120:             y = x.tan()\n2121:             try:\n2122:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2123:                 yield y\n2124:             finally:\n2125:                 pass\n2126: \n2127:         def f(x):\n2128:             y = x.sin()\n2129:             with whoo(x) as z:\n2130:                 y += z.neg()\n2131:             y += x.cos()\n2132:             return y\n2133: \n2134:         x = torch.randn(2)\n2135:         expected = f(x)\n2136:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2137:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n2138:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2139:         # no graph will be generated as we will skip all frames due to the graph break\n2140:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2141: \n2142:     def test_graph_break_inside_ctx_with_side_effects(self):\n2143:         L = []\n2144: \n2145:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2146:         def whoo(x):\n2147:             y = x.tan()\n2148:             try:\n2149:                 L.append(x.sin())\n2150:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2151:                 yield y\n2152:             finally:\n2153:                 L.append(x.cos())\n2154: \n2155:         def f(x):\n2156:             y = x.sin()\n2157:             with whoo(x) as z:\n2158:                 y += z.neg()\n2159:             y += x.cos()\n2160:             return y\n2161: \n2162:         x = torch.randn(2)\n2163:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2164:         y = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n2165:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin() + x.tan().neg() + x.cos())\n2166:         self.assertEqual(L, [x.sin(), x.cos()])\n2167:         # no graph will be generated as we will skip all frames due to the graph break\n2168:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2169: \n2170:     def test_graph_break_inside_ctx_1(self):\n2171:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2172:         def whoo(x):\n2173:             y = x.tan()\n2174:             try:\n2175:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2176:                 yield y\n2177:             finally:\n2178:                 pass\n2179: \n2180:         def bar(x):\n2181:             with whoo(x) as z:\n2182:                 return z.neg()\n2183: \n2184:         def f(x):\n2185:             return x.sin() + bar(x) + x.cos()\n2186: \n2187:         x = torch.randn(2)\n2188:         expected = f(x)\n2189:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2190:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n2191:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2192:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 2)\n2193:         self.assertExpectedInline(\n2194:             normalize_gm(eager.graphs[0].print_readable(False)),\n2195:             \"\"\"\\\n2196: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n2197:     def forward(self, L_x_: \"f32[2]\"):\n2198:         l_x_ = L_x_\n2199: \n2200:         sin: \"f32[2]\" = l_x_.sin();  l_x_ = None\n2201:         return (sin,)\n2202: \"\"\",\n2203:         )\n2204:         self.assertExpectedInline(\n2205:             normalize_gm(eager.graphs[1].print_readable(False)),\n2206:             \"\"\"\\\n2207: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n2208:     def forward(self, L_stack0_: \"f32[2]\", L_stack1_: \"f32[2]\", L_x_: \"f32[2]\"):\n2209:         l_stack0_ = L_stack0_\n2210:         l_stack1_ = L_stack1_\n2211:         l_x_ = L_x_\n2212: \n2213:         add: \"f32[2]\" = l_stack0_ + l_stack1_;  l_stack0_ = l_stack1_ = None\n2214:         cos: \"f32[2]\" = l_x_.cos();  l_x_ = None\n2215:         add_1: \"f32[2]\" = add + cos;  add = cos = None\n2216:         return (add_1,)\n2217: \"\"\",\n2218:         )\n2219: \n2220:     def test_graph_break_inside_ctx_2(self):\n2221:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2222:         def whoo(x):\n2223:             try:\n2224:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2225:                 yield x.cos()\n2226:             finally:\n2227:                 pass\n2228: \n2229:         def g(x):\n2230:             return x.neg() + x.acos()\n2231: \n2232:         def f(x):\n2233:             y = x.sin()\n2234:             with whoo(x) as z:\n2235:                 y += g(z)\n2236:             y += y.tan()\n2237:             return y\n2238: \n2239:         x = torch.randn(2)\n2240:         expected = f(x)\n2241:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2242:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n2243:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2244:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 1)\n2245: \n2246:     def test_graph_break_before___enter__(self):\n2247:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2248:         def whoo(x):\n2249:             try:\n2250:                 yield x + 1\n2251:             finally:\n2252:                 pass\n2253: \n2254:         def fn(x):\n2255:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2256:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2257:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2258:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2259:             return y\n2260: \n2261:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2262:         with self.assertRaises(InternalTorchDynamoError):\n2263:             torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(x)\n2264: \n2265:     def test_graph_break_in_finally(self):\n2266:         z = []\n2267: \n2268:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2269:         def whoo(x):\n2270:             nonlocal z\n2271:             try:\n2272:                 z.append(x)\n2273:                 yield x.sin()\n2274:             finally:\n2275:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2276:                 z.append(x.cos())\n2277: \n2278:         def fn(x):\n2279:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2280:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2281:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2282:             return y\n2283: \n2284:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2285:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2286:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2287:         self.assertEqual(out, x.sin())\n2288:         self.assertEqual(z, [x, x.cos()])\n2289:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2290: \n2291:     def test_graph_break_inside___enter__(self):\n2292:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2293:         def whoo(x):\n2294:             try:\n2295:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2296:                 yield x + 1\n2297:             finally:\n2298:                 pass\n2299: \n2300:         def fn(x):\n2301:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2302:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2303:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2304:             return y\n2305: \n2306:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2307:         expected = fn(x)\n2308: \n2309:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2310:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2311:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2312:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2313: \n2314:     def test_graph_break_after___enter__(self):\n2315:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2316:         def whoo(x):\n2317:             try:\n2318:                 yield x + 1\n2319:             finally:\n2320:                 pass\n2321: \n2322:         def fn(x):\n2323:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2324:             try:\n2325:                 y = ctx.__enter__()\n2326:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2327:             finally:\n2328:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2329:             return y\n2330: \n2331:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2332:         expected = fn(x)\n2333: \n2334:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2335:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2336:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2337:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2338: \n2339:     def test_graph_break_before_and_after___enter__(self):\n2340:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2341:         def whoo(x):\n2342:             try:\n2343:                 yield x + 1\n2344:             finally:\n2345:                 pass\n2346: \n2347:         def fn(x):\n2348:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2349:             try:\n2350:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2351:                 y = ctx.__enter__()\n2352:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2353:             finally:\n2354:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2355:             return y\n2356: \n2357:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2358:         expected = fn(x)\n2359: \n2360:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2361:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2362:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2363:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2364: \n2365:     def test_graph_break_before___enter___and_disable___exit__(self):\n2366:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2367:         def whoo(x):\n2368:             try:\n2369:                 yield x + 1\n2370:             finally:\n2371:                 pass\n2372: \n2373:         def fn(x):\n2374:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2375:             try:\n2376:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2377:                 y = ctx.__enter__()\n2378:             finally:\n2379: \n2380:                 @torch._dynamo.disable\n2381:                 def g():\n2382:                     ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2383: \n2384:                 g()\n2385:             return y\n2386: \n2387:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2388:         expected = fn(x)\n2389: \n2390:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2391:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2392:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2393:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2394: \n2395:     def test_disable___enter__(self):\n2396:         def h(x):\n2397:             return x.cos()\n2398: \n2399:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2400:         def whoo(x):\n2401:             try:\n2402:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2403:             finally:\n2404:                 pass\n2405: \n2406:         def fn(x):\n2407:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2408: \n2409:             @torch._dynamo.disable\n2410:             def g():\n2411:                 return ctx.__enter__()\n2412: \n2413:             y = g()\n2414:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2415:             return y\n2416: \n2417:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2418:         with self.assertRaises(InternalTorchDynamoError):\n2419:             torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(x)\n2420: \n2421:     def test_disable___exit__(self):\n2422:         def h(x):\n2423:             return x.cos()\n2424: \n2425:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2426:         def whoo(x):\n2427:             try:\n2428:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2429:             finally:\n2430:                 pass\n2431: \n2432:         def fn(x):\n2433:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2434:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2435: \n2436:             @torch._dynamo.disable\n2437:             def g():\n2438:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2439: \n2440:             g()\n2441: \n2442:             return y\n2443: \n2444:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2445:         with self.assertRaises(InternalTorchDynamoError):\n2446:             torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(x)\n2447: \n2448:     def test_contextmanager_as_argument(self):\n2449:         def h(x):\n2450:             return x.cos()\n2451: \n2452:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2453:         def whoo(x):\n2454:             try:\n2455:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2456:             finally:\n2457:                 pass\n2458: \n2459:         def fn(x, ctx):\n2460:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2461:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2462:             return x + y\n2463: \n2464:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2465:         expected = fn(x, whoo(x))\n2466: \n2467:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2468:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x, whoo(x))\n2469:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2470:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 2)\n2471: \n2472:     def test_return_new_contextmanager(self):\n2473:         L = []\n2474: \n2475:         def h(x):\n2476:             return x.cos()\n2477: \n2478:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2479:         def whoo(x):\n2480:             try:\n2481:                 L.append(x.sin())\n2482:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2483:             finally:\n2484:                 L.append(x.cos())\n2485: \n2486:
