      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "87: \n88: In general, resolving the graph break causing the skip will also resolve the skip.\n89: \n90: ## Graph Break in a Context Manager\n91: \n92: Another common example of an unresumable graph break is a graph break in most context managers:\n93: \n94: ",
            "endLine": 94,
            "startLine": 87
          },
          {
            "content": "121: \n122: There are some context managers where Dynamo can resume after a graph break.\n123: Some of these can be found in `supported_ctx_manager_classes` in `torch/_dynamo/variables/torch.py`.\n124: In general, any context manager represented by a `ContextWrappingVariable` subclass in\n125: `torch/_dynamo/variables/ctx_manager.py` support resuming after a graph break. For example:\n126: \n127: ",
            "endLine": 127,
            "startLine": 121
          },
          {
            "content": "108: \n109: We can avoid skipping by moving the graph break outside of the context manager:\n110: \n111: ",
            "endLine": 111,
            "startLine": 108
          }
        ],

        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",

      },

torch._C._set_grad_enabled(mode)\n60:     finally:\n61:         torch._C._set_grad_enabled(prev)\n62: \n63: \n64: class CustomizedCtxManagerWithGraphBreak(CustomizedCtxManager):\n65:     def __enter__(self):\n66:         torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n67:         super().__enter__()\n68: \n69: \n70: @contextlib.contextmanager\n71: def customized_ctx_manager_with_graph_break(mode):\n72:     prev = torch.is_grad_enabled()\n73:     try:\n74:         torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n75:         yield torch._C._set_grad_enabled(mode)\n76:     finally:\n77:         torch._C._set_grad_enabled(prev)\n78: \n79: \n80: class CtxManagerTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\n81:     def test_no_grad(self):\n82:         def fn1(a, b):\n83:             x = a + 1\n84:             # redundant no_grad should get ignored\n85:             with torch.no_grad():\n86:                 x = x + b\n87:             x = x + 2\n88:             return x\n89: \n90:         def fn2(a, b):\n91:             x = a + 1\n92:             with torch.set_grad_enabled(False):\n93:                 x = x + b\n94:             x = x + 2\n95:             return x\n96: \n97:         def fn3(a, b):\n98:             x = a + 1\n99:             with torch.enable_grad():\n100:                 x = x + b\n101: ",

remap original instructions' exception table entries\\n547:             if old_hook_target_remap:\\n548:                 assert is_py311_plus\\n549:                 for inst in instructions:\\n550:                     if (\\n551:                         inst.exn_tab_entry\\n552:                         and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\\n553:                     ):\\n554:                         inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n555:                             inst.exn_tab_entry.target\\n556:                         ]\\n557: \\n558:             # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\\n559:             instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\\n560: \\n561:         new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\\n562:         ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\\n563:         return new_code\\n564: \\n565:     @staticmethod\\n566:     def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\\n567:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n568:         return [\\n569:             create_load_const(None),\\n570:             create_instruction(\\\"RAISE_VARARGS\\\", arg=1),\\n571:         ]\\n572: \\n573:     @classmethod\\n574:     def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\\n575:         cls,\\n576:         code: types.CodeType,\\n577:         lineno: int,\\n578:         offset: int,\\n579:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\\n580:         *args: Any,\\n581:     ) -> types.CodeType:\\n582:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n583:         This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\\n584:         to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\\n585:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\\n586:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\\n587:         resume functions).\\n588:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n589: \\n590:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\\n591:             code\\n592:         ]\\n593:         new_offset = -1\\n594: \\n595:         def find_new_offset(\\n596:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n597:         ) -> None:\\n598:             nonlocal new_offset\\n599:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\\n601:             (new_target,) = (\\n602:                 i2\\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\\n604:                 if i1 is target\\n605:             )\\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\\n609: \\n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\\n612: \\n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\\n616:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\\n617:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\\n618:             if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\\n619:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\\n620:                     new_offset\\n621:                 ] = {}\\n622: \\n623:                 def remap_block_offsets(\\n624:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n625:                 ) -> None:\\n626:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\\n627:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\\n628:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\\n629:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\\n630:                     # replaced.\\n631:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\\n632:                     for inst in instructions:\\n633:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\\n634:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n635:                         ):\\n636:                             break\\n637:                         if inst.opname == \\\"PUSH_EXC_INFO\\\":\\n638:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\\n639: \\n640:                     # offsets into prefix\\n641:                     for inst, o in zip(\\n642:                         prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n643:                     ):\\n644:                         block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\\n645: \\n646:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\\n647:                     old_start_offset = (\\n648:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\\n649:                     )\\n650:                     # offsets into old bytecode\\n651:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\\n652:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\\n653:                     )\\n654:                     targets = _filter_iter(\\n655:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\\n656:                     )\\n657:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\\n658:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\\n659:                         targets,\\n660:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\\n661:                     )\\n662:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\\n663:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\\n664: \\n665:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\\n666: \\n667:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\\n668:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\\n669:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\\n670:                 for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\\n671:             )\\n672:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\\n673:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\\n674:         )\\n675: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py%3FL540-675\"}",

torch.cuda.current_stream()\\n311:             new_stream.wait_stream(cur_stream)\\n312: \\n313:             with torch.cuda.stream(new_stream):\\n314:                 x = torch.sin(x)\\n315: Show me:\n- Any test function that involves nested context managers\n- Any test that has graph breaks inside with blocks\n- Any test involving STORE_ATTR in a with block\n\nFocus on file paths: test/dynamo/test_repros.py and test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a9597865b31fc36f5",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary of all the relevant test functions:\n\n## Summary of Test Functions Related to Context Managers, Graph Breaks, and STORE_ATTR\n\nBased on my search of the PyTorch dynamo test suite, here are the test functions organized by category:\n\n### **In test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py:**\n\n#### 1. **Nested Context Managers with Graph Breaks:**\n\n**`test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break`** (Lines 148-168)\n- Tests nested `torch.set_grad_enabled()` context managers with graph breaks at multiple levels\n- Verifies that grad mode state is preserved correctly across graph breaks\n- Uses `torch._dynamo.graph_break()` inside nested with blocks\n- Expected frame count: 2\n\n**`test_nested_generic_context_manager_with_graph_break`** (Lines 1176-1200)\n- Tests nested custom context managers (CustomizedCtxManager) with graph breaks\n- Nested structure: outer `with Ctx(True)`, inner `with Ctx(False)` with graph break\n- Has graph break inside nested with block at line 1185\n- Expected frame counts vary based on context manager type\n\n**`test_autocast_graph_break_nested`** (Lines 800-840)\n- Tests deeply nested `torch.autocast()` context managers (3 levels)\n- Multiple graph breaks at different nesting levels\n- Complex scenario: autocast -> autocast disabled -> autocast enabled\n- Tests that dtype is correctly remembered after graph breaks in nested contexts\n\n**`test_311_resume_block_keyerror2`** (Lines 1875-1886)\n- Tests nested `torch.no_grad()` with graph breaks between them\n- Minimal reproduction of issue #166176\n- Graph break occurs inside nested with blocks\n\n#### 2. **Context Managers with Graph Breaks (non-nested):**\n\n**`test_torch_profiler`** (Lines 170-188)\n- Nested `torch.profiler.profile()` and `torch.profiler.record_function()`\n- Has `z.tolist()` which causes implicit graph break at line 178\n- Tests nested profiler context managers with graph breaks\n\n**`test_autograd_profiler`** (Lines 190-208)\n- Similar to test_torch_profiler but with `torch.autograd.profiler`\n- Nested profiler and record_function calls\n- Graph break from `z.tolist()` inside nested with blocks\n\n**`test_generic_context_manager_with_graph_break`** (Lines 1140-1168)\n- Single-level context manager with explicit graph break inside\n- Tests CustomizedCtxManager with graph break at line 1146\n\n**`test_return_context_manager_with_graph_break`** (Lines 1051-1062)\n- Context manager returned after a graph break\n- Graph break happens before entering the with block\n\n**`test_311_resume_block_keyerror`** (Lines 1851-1873)\n- Tests `torch.no_grad()` with graph breaks in conditional branches\n- Graph breaks inside with blocks in if/else branches\n\n**`test_torch_profiler_use_after_with_block`** (Lines 1835-1849)\n- Tests using the context manager variable after exiting the with block\n- Has graph break from `p.profiler.kineto_results.experimental_event_tree()` at line 1841\n\n#### 3. **STORE_ATTR in with blocks:**\n\n**`test_store_attr_graph_break_key_error`** (Lines 1888-1902)\n- STORE_ATTR operation (`dummy.attr1 = x`) inside a `torch.no_grad()` context\n- The STORE_ATTR causes a graph break\n- Verifies that graph_break counter is incremented\n\n---\n\n### **In test/dynamo/test_repros.py:**\n\n#### 1. **Nested Context Managers with Graph Breaks:**\n\n**`test_with_on_graph_break_nested`** (Lines 1923-1947)\n- Tests nested `torch.no_grad()` and `torch.enable_grad()` context managers\n- Graph break occurs inside a function call within nested with blocks\n- Function `reversible()` has explicit `torch._dynamo.graph_break()` at line 1925\n- Tests interaction between nested context managers and graph breaks\n\n**`test_with_on_graph_break_inst`** (Lines 1899-1921)\n- Tests single-level `torch.enable_grad()` with graph break from function call\n- Function `reversible()` has implicit graph break from `print()` statement\n- Similar structure to nested test but without outer context manager\n\n#### 2. **Graph Breaks with State Preservation:**\n\n**`test_grad_mode_carrying_correct_state_after_graph_break`** (Lines 1950-1962)\n- Tests that `torch.no_grad()` correctly carries state across graph breaks\n- Graph break from `print(\"Break\")` inside with block\n- Verifies that `requires_grad` attribute is correctly preserved after graph break\n\n#### 3. **Context Manager Constructor with Graph Breaks:**\n\n**`test_user_ctor_ctx_manager_custom_init_graph_break`** (Lines 4656-1685)\n- Tests user-defined context manager class instantiation\n- Context manager constructor is called without explicit with block usage\n- Tests counter behavior with context managers that have custom `__init__`\n\n#### 4. **Other Related Tests:**\n\n**`test_abc_setattr`** (Lines 1964-1995)\n- Tests STORE_ATTR operations on module attributes\n- `self.foo = 0` causes graph break\n- Tests `__setattr__` and attribute assignment behavior\n\n---\n\n### **Key Patterns to Note:**\n\n1. **Nested with blocks:** Most tests have 2-3 levels of nesting\n2. **Graph break placement:** Breaks occur either:\n   - Directly inside with blocks (explicit `torch._dynamo.graph_break()`)\n   - From function calls that contain graph breaks\n   - From implicit breaks (`.tolist()`, `.print()`, `__setattr__`)\n3. **STORE_ATTR context:** Only `test_store_attr_graph_break_key_error` explicitly tests STORE_ATTR in a with block\n4. **State preservation:** Tests verify that context manager state (grad mode, dtype, etc.) is correctly maintained across graph breaks\n\nThese tests are critical for ensuring that the dynamo compiler correctly handles the interaction between Python context managers and graph breaking operations.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],

"{\"content\":\"1175:     )\\n1176:     def test_nested_generic_context_manager_with_graph_break(self, Ctx):\\n1177:         def fn(x):\\n1178:             with Ctx(True):\\n1179:                 x = x + 1\\n1180:                 if torch.is_grad_enabled():\\n1181:                     x = x * 2\\n1182:                 with Ctx(False):\\n1183:                     if torch.is_grad_enabled():\\n1184:                         x = x - 3\\n1185:                     torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n1186:                     x = x * 1.5\\n1187:                 x = torch.relu(x)\\n1188:             return x - 1\\n1189: \\n1190:         x = torch.rand(2, 3)\\n1191:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n1192:         opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=cnts, fullgraph=False)(fn)\\n1193: \\n1194:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1195:             ref = fn(x)\\n1196:             res = opt_fn(x)\\n1197:             self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1198:             if Ctx is CustomizedCtxManager:\\n1199:                 self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 4)\\n1200:                 self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 4)\\n1201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py%3FL1175-1200\"}"

"{\"content\":\"148:     def test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break(self):\\n149:         def fn(x):\\n150:             before = torch.is_grad_enabled()\\n151:             with torch.set_grad_enabled(False):\\n152:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n153:                 with torch.set_grad_enabled(True):\\n154:                     x = torch.mul(x, 5)\\n155:                     torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n156:                     x = torch.sqrt(x)\\n157:                     assert torch.is_grad_enabled()  # noqa: S101\\n158:                 assert not torch.is_grad_enabled()  # noqa: S101\\n159:             assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == before  # noqa: S101\\n160:             return x\\n161: \\n162:         a = torch.randn([3, 4])\\n163:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n164:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\\n165: \\n166:         for _ in range(10):\\n167:             opt_fn(a)\\n168:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\\n169: \\n170:     def test_torch_profiler(self):\\n171:         # wrap torch.profiler.* as NullContextVariable and do nothing\\n172:         def fn(x):\\n173:             y = x**2\\n174:             with torch.profiler.profile():\\n175:                 y = y + 2\\n176:                 with torch.profiler.record_function(\\\"my_function\\\"):\\n177:                     z = y**3\\n178:                     z.tolist()  # graph break\\n179:                     z = z + 1\\n180:             return z\\n181: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py%3FL148-180\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1888:     def test_store_attr_graph_break_key_error(self):\\n1889:         # STORE_ATTR on dummy should result in graph break\\n1890:         def dummy():\\n1891:             pass\\n1892: \\n1893:         def fn(x):\\n1894:             x = x + 2\\n1895:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1896:                 dummy.attr1 = x\\n1897:             return x + 4\\n1898: \\n1899:         inp = torch.ones(3)\\n1900:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1901:         self.assertEqual(fn(inp), opt_fn(inp))\\n1902:         self.assertGreater(len(counters[\\\"graph_break\\\"]), 0)\\n1903: \\n1904: \\n1905: class ContextlibContextManagerTests(\\n1906:     torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCaseWithNestedGraphBreaks\\n1907: ):\\n1908:     def setUp(self):\\n1909:         super().setUp()\\n1910:         self._prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib\\n1911:         self._u_prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest\\n1912:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = True\\n1913:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = True\\n1914: \\n1915:     def tearDown(self):\\n1916:         super().tearDown()\\n1917:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = self._prev\\n1918:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = self._u_prev\\n1919: \\n1920:     def test_ctx_basic0(self):\\n1921: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py%3FL1888-1920\"}"

"{\"content\":\"810:                     ):\\n811:                         torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n812:                         g_float32 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\\n813:                         with torch.autocast(device_type=\\\"cpu\\\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\\n814:                             # Check that nested with non-inlineable function with graph break\\n815:                             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n816:                             f_float16_1 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\\n817:                     # We remember to exit the inner autocast correctly to outer\\n818:                     # even after graph breaks\\n819:                     f_float16 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\\n820:                     assert f_float16.dtype == f_float16_1.dtype  # noqa: S101\\n821:                 return f_float16, g_float32\\n822: \\n823:         module = MyModule()\\n824:         real_16, real_32 = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\\n825:         real_device_16 = real_16.device\\n826:         real_dtype_16 = real_16.dtype\\n827:         real_device_32 = real_32.device\\n828:         real_dtype_32 = real_32.dtype\\n829: \\n830:         graph = torch.compile(module, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n831:         out_16, out_32 = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\\n832:         self.assertEqual(out_16.device, real_device_16)\\n833:         self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, real_dtype_16)\\n834:         self.assertEqual(out_32.device, real_device_32)\\n835:         self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, real_dtype_32)\\n836: \\n837:         self.assertEqual(out_16.device.type, \\\"cpu\\\")\\n838:         self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\\n839:         self.assertEqual(out_32.device.type, \\\"cpu\\\")\\n840:         self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, torch.float32)\\n841: \\n842:     def test_autocast_graph_break_method(self):\\n843:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\\n844:             def __init__(self, bias):\\n845:                 super().__init__()\\n846:                 self.bias = bias\\n847: \\n848:             def mm_not_break(self, x, y):\\n849:                 return torch.mm(x, y) + self.bias\\n850: \\n851: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py%3FL810-850\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1920:             res = opt_fn(x)\\n1921:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1922: \\n1923:     def test_with_on_graph_break_nested(self):\\n1924:         def reversible(x):\\n1925:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()  # Cause graph break so inline fails\\n1926:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\\n1927: \\n1928:         def fn(x):\\n1929:             # nested context manager failed previously\\n1930:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1931:                 with torch.enable_grad():\\n1932:                     a = torch.sin(x)\\n1933:                     b = reversible(a)\\n1934:                     c = torch.sigmoid(b)\\n1935:                     c.sum().backward()\\n1936:                     return x.grad\\n1937: \\n1938:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1939:         x.grad = None\\n1940:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1941:             ref = fn(x)\\n1942: \\n1943:         x.grad = None\\n1944:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1945:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1946:             res = opt_fn(x)\\n1947:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1948: \\n1949:     # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1446\\n1950:     def test_grad_mode_carrying_correct_state_after_graph_break(self):\\n1951: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL1920-1950\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1051:     def test_return_context_manager_with_graph_break(self):\\n1052:         @torch.compile(backend=\\\"eager\\\", fullgraph=False)\\n1053:         def f(x):\\n1054:             cm = CustomizedCtxManager(False)\\n1055:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n1056:             with cm:\\n1057:                 pass\\n1058:             return cm\\n1059: \\n1060:         x = torch.randn(2, 3)\\n1061:         cm = f(x)\\n1062:         self.assertFalse(cm.mode)\\n1063: \\n1064:     @torch._dynamo.config.patch(enable_trace_contextlib=True)\\n1065:     @parametrize(\\n1066:         \\\"Ctx\\\",\\n1067:         [CustomizedCtxManager, customized_ctx_manager],\\n1068:         name_fn=lambda x: x.__name__,\\n1069:     )\\n1070:     def test_generic_context_manager(self, Ctx):\\n1071:         def fn(x):\\n1072:             with Ctx(True):\\n1073:                 x = x + 1\\n1074:                 if torch.is_grad_enabled():\\n1075:                     x = x * 2\\n1076:                 x = torch.relu(x)\\n1077:             return x - 1\\n1078: \\n1079:         x = torch.rand(2, 3)\\n1080:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n1081: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py%3FL1051-1080\"}"

"{\"content\":\"3029:     def test_nested_while_loop_graph_break(self):\\n3030:         def inner_loop(x):\\n3031:             i = 3\\n3032:             while i > 0:\\n3033:                 i -= 1\\n3034:                 x += 1\\n3035:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n3036:             return x\\n3037: \\n3038:         def inner(x):\\n3039:             inner_loop(x)\\n3040:             return torch.sin(x)\\n3041: \\n3042:         def fn(x):\\n3043:             i = 20\\n3044:             while i > 10:\\n3045:                 x = inner(x)\\n3046:                 i -= 1\\n3047:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n3048:             return x\\n3049: \\n3050:         cnt = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n3051:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnt)\\n3052:         x = torch.randn(4)\\n3053:         opt_fn(x)\\n3054:         self.assertEqual(cnt.frame_count, 1)\\n3055:         self.assertEqual(cnt.op_count, 1)\\n3056: \\n3057:     def test_while_loop_graph_break_inside_call_function(self):\\n3058:         # Repro of huggingface graph break inside loop in `get_parameter_dtype`.\\n3059:         # Skip only the inner frame that has loop that contains graph break.\\n3060:         def inner(x):\\n3061:             for _ in range(3):\\n3062:                 x += 1\\n3063:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n3064:             return x\\n3065: \\n3066:         def fn(x):\\n3067:             x += 2\\n3068:             inner(x)\\n3069:             x += 3\\n3070:             return x\\n3071: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL3029-3070\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: # Owner(s): [\\\"module: dynamo\\\"]\\n2: import contextlib\\n3: import sys\\n4: import unittest\\n5: from contextlib import contextmanager\\n6: \\n7: import torch\\n8: import torch._dynamo.test_case\\n9: import torch._dynamo.testing\\n10: from torch._dynamo.testing import EagerAndRecordGraphs, normalize_gm, same\\n11: from torch._dynamo.utils import counters\\n12: from torch.nn import functional as F\\n13: from torch.testing._internal.common_cuda import PLATFORM_SUPPORTS_FLASH_ATTENTION\\n14: from torch.testing._internal.common_utils import (\\n15:     instantiate_parametrized_tests,\\n16:     parametrize,\\n17: )\\n18: \\n19: \\n20: try:\\n21:     from . import test_functions\\n22: except ImportError:\\n23:     import test_functions\\n24: \\n25: \\n26: _variable = 0\\n27: _variable1 = 0\\n28: z_glb = 0\\n29: k_glb = 0\\n30: \\n31: \\n32: @contextlib.contextmanager\\n33: def set_default_dtype(dtype):\\n34:     old_dtype = "{\"content\":\"50:     def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback):\\n51:         torch._C._set_grad_enabled(self.prev)\\n52: \\n53: \\n54: @contextlib.contextmanager\\n55: def customized_ctx_manager(mode):\\n56:     prev = torch.is_grad_enabled()\\n57:     try:\\n58:         yield torch._C._set_grad_enabled(mode)\\n59:     finally:\\n60:         torch._C._set_grad_enabled(prev)\\n61: \\n62: \\n63: class CustomizedCtxManagerWithGraphBreak(CustomizedCtxManager):\\n64:     def __enter__(self):\\n65:         torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n66:         super().__enter__()\\n67: \\n68: \\n69: @contextlib.contextmanager\\n70: def customized_ctx_manager_with_graph_break(mode):\\n71:     prev = torch.is_grad_enabled()\\n72:     try:\\n73:         torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n74:         yield torch._C._set_grad_enabled(mode)\\n75:     finally:\\n76:         torch._C._set_grad_enabled(prev)\\n77: \\n78: \\n79: class CtxManagerTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCaseWithNestedGraphBreaks):\\n80:     def test_no_grad(self):\\n81:         def fn1(a, b):\\n82:             x = a + 1\\n83:             # redundant no_grad should get ignored\\n84:             with torch.no_grad():\\n85:                 x = x + b\\n86:             x = x + 2\\n87:             return x\\n88: \\n89:         def fn2(a, b):\\n90:             x = a + 1\\n91:             with torch.set_grad_enabled(False):\\n92:                 x = x + b\\n93:             x = x + 2\\n94:             return x\\n95: \\n96:         def fn3(a, b):\\n97:             x = a + 1\\n98:             with torch.enable_grad():\\n99:                 x = x + b\\n100:             x = x + 2\\n101:             return x\\n102: \\n103:         def fn4(a, b):\\n104:             x = a + 1\\n105:             with torch.set_grad_enabled(True):\\n106:                 if torch.is_grad_enabled():\\n107:                     x = x + b\\n108:             x = x + 2\\n109:             return x\\n110: \\n111:         with torch.no_grad():\\n112:             torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(\\n113:                 self, fn=fn1, nargs=2, expected_ops=3\\n114:             )  # coalesced noop\\n115:             torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(\\n116:                 self, fn=fn2, nargs=2, expected_ops=3\\n117:             )  # coalesced noop\\n118:             torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn3, nargs=2, expected_ops=5)\\n119:             torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn4, nargs=2, expected_ops=5)\\n120:         with torch.enable_grad():\\n121:             torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn1, nargs=2, expected_ops=5)\\n122:             torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn2, nargs=2, expected_ops=5)\\n123:             torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(\\n124:                 self, fn=fn3, nargs=2, expected_ops=3\\n125:             )  # coalesced noop\\n126:             torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(\\n127:                 self, fn=fn4, nargs=2, expected_ops=3\\n128:             )  # coalesced noop\\n129: \\n130:     def test_grad_mode_guard(self):\\n131:         def fn(a, b):\\n132:             prev_grad = torch.is_grad_enabled()\\n133:             torch.set_grad_enabled(False)\\n134:             a = a + 1\\n135:             a.tolist()  # "{\"content\":\"170:     def test_torch_profiler(self):\\n171:         # wrap torch.profiler.* as NullContextVariable and do nothing\\n172:         def fn(x):\\n173:             y = x**2\\n174:             with torch.profiler.profile():\\n175:                 y = y + 2\\n176:                 with torch.profiler.record_function(\\\"my_function\\\"):\\n177:                     z = y**3\\n178:                     z.tolist()  # graph break\\n179:                     z = z + 1\\n180:             return z\\n181: \\n182:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), requires_grad=True)\\n183:         ref = fn(x)\\n184:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n185:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\\n186:         res = opt_fn(x)\\n187:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n188:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\\n189: \\n190:     def test_autograd_profiler(self):\\n191:         # wrap torch.autograd.profiler.* as NullContextVariable and do nothing\\n192:         def fn(x):\\n193:             y = x**2\\n194:             with torch.autograd.profiler.profile():\\n195:                 y = y + 2\\n196:                 with torch.autograd.profiler.record_function(\\\"my_function\\\"):\\n197:                     z = y**3\\n198:                     z.tolist()  # graph break\\n199:                     z = z + 1\\n200:             return z\\n201: \\n202:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), requires_grad=True)\\n203:         ref = fn(x)\\n204:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n205:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\\n206:         res = opt_fn(x)\\n207:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n208:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\\n209: \\n210:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \\\"requires cuda\\\")\\n211: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/pytorch/pytorch@d7e39661894994643e0436d4f777e1865fb096bf/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py%3FL170-210\"}"

https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/162313\\n1853:         flag = True\\n1854: \\n1855:         def fn(x):\\n1856:             x = x + 1\\n1857:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n1858:             x = x + 2\\n1859:             if flag:\\n1860:                 with torch.no_grad():\\n1861:                     torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n1862:                 x = x + 4\\n1863:             else:\\n1864:                 with torch.no_grad():\\n1865:                     torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n1866:                 x = x + 8\\n1867:             return x + 16\\n1868: \\n1869:         inp = torch.ones(3)\\n1870:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1871:         self.assertEqual(fn(inp), opt_fn(inp))\\n1872:         flag = False\\n1873:         self.assertEqual(fn(inp), opt_fn(inp))\\n1874: \\n1875:     def test_311_resume_block_keyerror2(self):\\n1876:         # "{\"content\":\"4656:     def test_user_ctor_ctx_manager_custom_init_graph_break(self):\\n4657:         counter = [0]\\n4658: \\n4659:         class UserCtxManager:\\n4660:             def __init__(self, k):\\n4661:                 k[0] += 1\\n4662: \\n4663:             def __enter__(self):\\n4664:                 return 1\\n4665: \\n4666:             def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):\\n4667:                 pass\\n4668: \\n4669:         def fn(x, counter):\\n4670:             x = x * x\\n4671:             ucm = UserCtxManager(counter)  # "{\"content\":\"1950:     def test_grad_mode_carrying_correct_state_after_graph_break(self):\\n1951:         def fn(x):\\n1952:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1953:                 y = x * 3\\n1954:                 print(\\\"Break\\\")\\n1955:                 z = x + 2\\n1956:             return y, z\\n1957: \\n1958:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1959:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1960:         y, z = opt_fn(x)\\n1961:         self.assertFalse(y.requires_grad)\\n1962:         self.assertFalse(z.requires_grad)\\n1963: \\n1964:     def test_abc_setattr(self):\\n1965:         # tests that we correctly bail out of __setattr__ calls\\n1966: \\n1967:         # TODO: does not ensure ABC classes are correctly inferred as ClassVariables\\n1968:         # (doesn't test the fix for 'super()')\\n1969: \\n1970:         class BaseModule(torch.nn.Module, ABC):\\n1971:             def blah(self, x):\\n1972:                 return x + 1\\n1973: \\n1974:         class Derived(BaseModule):\\n1975:             def __setattr__(self, name, value) -> None:\\n1976:                 super().__setattr__(name, value)\\n1977: \\n1978:             def forward(self, x):\\n1979:                 # expect a graph break on __setattr__\\n1980:                 self.foo = 0\\n1981:                 return self.blah(x)\\n1982: \\n1983:             def blah(self, x):\\n1984:                 return super().blah(x)\\n1985: \\n1986:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1987:         mod = Derived()\\n1988:         opt_mod = torch.compile(mod, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1989:         opt_mod(x)\\n1990: \\n1991:         # Not sure what this test is testing.  set_default_dtype = None\\n1879: \\n1880:         x: \\\"c128[2]\\\" = torch.tensor([3.0, (3+5j)])\\n1881: \\n1882:         set_default_dtype_1 = torch.set_default_dtype(torch.float32);  set_default_dtype_1 = None\\n1883:         return (x,)\\n1884: \\\"\\\"\\\",\\n1885:         )\\n1886: \\n1887:     def test_ctx_basic1(self):\\n1888:         @contextlib.contextmanager\\n1889:         def compute_sin(x):\\n1890:             try:\\n1891:                 yield x.sin()\\n1892:             finally:\\n1893:                 pass\\n1894: \\n1895:         @torch.compile(backend=\\\"eager\\\", fullgraph=True)\\n1896:         def fn(x):\\n1897:             with compute_sin(x) as y:\\n1898:                 return y.cos()\\n1899: \\n1900:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\\n1901:         y = fn(x)\\n1902:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\\n1903: \\n1904:     def test_change_parent_nonlocal_0(self):\\n1905:         # test if a nonlocal actually gets propagated\\n1906:         z = 0\\n1907:         k = 0\\n1908: \\n1909:         def create_ctx():\\n1910:             @contextmanager\\n1911: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py%3FL1851-1910\"}",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1829: \\n1830:     def test_with_on_graph_break_inst(self):\\n1831:         def reversible(x):\\n1832:             print(\\\"Hello world\\\")  # Cause graph break so inline fails\\n1833:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\\n1834: \\n1835:         def fn(x):\\n1836:             with torch.enable_grad():\\n1837:                 a = torch.sin(x)\\n1838:                 b = reversible(a)\\n1839:                 c = torch.sigmoid(b)\\n1840:                 c.sum().backward()\\n1841:                 return x.grad\\n1842: \\n1843:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1844:         x.grad = None\\n1845:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1846:             ref = fn(x)\\n1847: \\n1848:         x.grad = None\\n1849:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1850:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1851:             res = opt_fn(x)\\n1852:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1853: \\n1854:     def test_with_on_graph_break_nested(self):\\n1855:         def reversible(x):\\n1856:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()  # Cause graph break so inline fails\\n1857:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\\n1858: \\n1859:         def fn(x):\\n1860:             # nested context manager failed previously\\n1861:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1862:                 with torch.enable_grad():\\n1863:                     a = torch.sin(x)\\n1864:                     b = reversible(a)\\n1865:                     c = torch.sigmoid(b)\\n1866:                     c.sum().backward()\\n1867:                     return x.grad\\n1868: \\n1869:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1870:         x.grad = None\\n1871:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1872:             ref = fn(x)\\n1873: \\n1874:         x.grad = None\\n1875:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1876:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1877:             res = opt_fn(x)\\n1878:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1879: \\n1880:     # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1446\\n1881:     def test_grad_mode_carrying_correct_state_after_graph_break(self):\\n1882:         def fn(x):\\n1883:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1884:                 y = x * 3\\n1885:                 print(\\\"Break\\\")\\n1886: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL1829-1885\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1829: \n1830:     def test_with_on_graph_break_inst(self):\n1831:         def reversible(x):\n1832:             print(\"Hello world\")  # Cause graph break so inline fails\n1833:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\n1834: \n1835:         def fn(x):\n1836:             with torch.enable_grad():\n1837:                 a = torch.sin(x)\n1838:                 b = reversible(a)\n1839:                 c = torch.sigmoid(b)\n1840:                 c.sum().backward()\n1841:                 return x.grad\n1842: \n1843:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n1844:         x.grad = None\n1845:         with torch.no_grad():\n1846:             ref = fn(x)\n1847: \n1848:         x.grad = None\n1849:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n1850:         with torch.no_grad():\n1851:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1852:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1853: \n1854:     def test_with_on_graph_break_nested(self):\n1855:         def reversible(x):\n1856:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()  # Cause graph break so inline fails\n1857:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\n1858: \n1859:         def fn(x):\n1860:             # nested context manager failed previously\n1861:             with torch.no_grad():\n1862:                 with torch.enable_grad():\n1863:                     a = torch.sin(x)\n1864:                     b = reversible(a)\n1865:                     c = torch.sigmoid(b)\n1866:                     c.sum().backward()\n1867:                     return x.grad\n1868: \n1869:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n1870:         x.grad = None\n1871:         with torch.no_grad():\n1872:             ref = fn(x)\n1873: \n1874:         x.grad = None\n1875:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n1876:         with torch.no_grad():\n1877:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1878:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1879: \n1880:     # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1446\n1881:     def test_grad_mode_carrying_correct_state_after_graph_break(self):\n1882:         def fn(x):\n1883:             with torch.no_grad():\n1884:                 y = x * 3\n1885:                 print(\"Break\")\n1886: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL1829-1885"
