"    56\u2192def _initial_push_null(insts: list[Instruction]) -> None:\n    57\u2192    if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n    58\u2192        insts.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n    59\u2192        if sys.version_info < (3, 13):\n    60\u2192            insts.append(create_instruction(\"SWAP\", arg=2))\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192# Generates bytecode from template and splits the code where LOAD_FAST dummy is present.\n    64\u2192def _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n    65\u2192    template: Callable[..., Any],\n    66\u2192    stack_index: int,\n    67\u2192    varname_map: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\n    68\u2192) -> tuple[list[Instruction], list[Instruction]]:\n    69\u2192    template_code = bytecode_from_template(template, varname_map=varname_map)\n    70\u2192    template_code.append(create_instruction(\"POP_TOP\"))\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192    # adjust exception table entry depth\n    73\u2192    for inst in template_code:\n    74\u2192        if inst.exn_tab_entry:\n    75\u2192            inst.exn_tab_entry.depth += stack_index\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192    # search for LOAD_FAST dummy and replace it with 2 NOPs (we can break up the bytecode between them)\n    78\u2192    dummy_idx, dummy_inst = next(\n    79\u2192        (\n    80\u2192            (i, inst)\n    81\u2192            for i, inst in enumerate(template_code)\n    82\u2192            if inst.opname == \"LOAD_FAST\" and inst.argval == \"dummy\"\n    83\u2192        ),\n    84\u2192        (None, None),\n    85\u2192    )\n    86\u2192    assert dummy_idx is not None and dummy_inst is not None\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192    # replace LOAD_FAST dummy with first NOP marking exception area\n    89\u2192    overwrite_instruction(dummy_inst, [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192    # POP_TOP follows LOAD_FAST dummy - replace with NOP marking end of exception area\n    92\u2192    assert template_code[dummy_idx + 1].opname == \"POP_TOP\"\n    93\u2192    overwrite_instruction(template_code[dummy_idx + 1], [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192    return template_code[: dummy_idx + 1], template_code[dummy_idx + 1 :]\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192def _try_except_tf_mode_template(dummy: Any, stack_var_name: Any) -> None:\n    99\u2192    # NOTE: Make sure this name matches what is generated by symbolic_convert:import_source\n   100\u2192    # on torch._dynamo.utils.\n   101\u2192    global __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils\n   102\u2192    try:\n   103\u2192        dummy\n   104\u2192    except:  # noqa: E722, B001\n   105\u2192        __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils.set_torch_function_mode_stack(  # type: ignore[name-defined]\n   106\u2192            stack_var_name\n   107\u2192        )\n   108\u2192        raise\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\n   112\u2192class ReenterWith:\n   113\u2192    stack_index: int\n   114\u2192    target_values: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]] = None\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    def try_except_torch_function_mode(\n   117\u2192        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n   118\u2192    ) -> list[Instruction]:\n   119\u2192        \"\"\"\n   120\u2192        Codegen based off of:\n   121\u2192        try:\n   122\u2192            (rest)\n   123\u2192        except:\n   124\u2192            (restore previous tf mode stack)\n   125\u2192            raise\n   126\u2192        \"\"\"\n   127\u2192        from .variables.torch_function import get_prev_stack_var_name\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192        setup_try_except, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n   130\u2192            _try_except_tf_mode_template,\n   131\u2192            self.stack_index,\n   132\u2192            varname_map={\"stack_var_name\": get_prev_stack_var_name()},\n   133\u2192        )\n   134\u2192        cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192        return setup_try_except\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    # If we do not want to destroy the stack, we can do the same thing as a\n   139\u2192    # `SETUP_WITH` block, only that we store the context manager in a local_symbol\n   140\u2192    def try_finally(\n   141\u2192        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n   142\u2192    ) -> list[Instruction]:\n   143\u2192        \"\"\"\n   144\u2192        Codegen based off of:\n   145\u2192        load args\n   146\u2192        enter context\n   147\u2192        try:\n   148\u2192            (rest)\n   149\u2192        finally:\n   150\u2192            exit context\n   151\u2192        \"\"\"\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   540\u2192                    inst.positions = None\n   541\u2192\n   542\u2192            if cleanup:\n   543\u2192                prefix.extend(cleanup)\n   544\u2192                prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\n   545\u2192\n   546\u2192            # remap original instructions' exception table entries\n   547\u2192            if old_hook_target_remap:\n   548\u2192                assert is_py311_plus\n   549\u2192                for inst in instructions:\n   550\u2192                    if (\n   551\u2192                        inst.exn_tab_entry\n   552\u2192                        and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\n   553\u2192                    ):\n   554\u2192                        inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   555\u2192                            inst.exn_tab_entry.target\n   556\u2192                        ]\n   557\u2192\n   558\u2192            # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\n   559\u2192            instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\n   560\u2192\n   561\u2192        new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\n   562\u2192        ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\n   563\u2192        return new_code\n   564\u2192\n   565\u2192    @staticmethod\n   566\u2192    def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\n   567\u2192        \"\"\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\"\"\"\n   568\u2192        return [\n   569\u2192            create_load_const(None),\n   570\u2192            create_instruction(\"RAISE_VARARGS\", arg=1),\n   571\u2192        ]\n   572\u2192\n   573\u2192    @classmethod\n   574\u2192    def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n   575\u2192        cls,\n   576\u2192        code: types.CodeType,\n   577\u2192        lineno: int,\n   578\u2192        offset: int,\n   579\u2192        setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\n   580\u2192        *args: Any,\n   581\u2192    ) -> types.CodeType:\n   582\u2192        \"\"\"\n   583\u2192        This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\n   584\u2192        to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\n   585\u2192        from the original code object so that if control flow paths\n   586\u2192        converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\n   587\u2192        resume functions).\n   588\u2192        \"\"\"\n   589\u2192\n   590\u2192        meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\n   591\u2192            code\n   592\u2192        ]\n   593\u2192        new_offset = -1\n   594\u2192\n   595\u2192        def find_new_offset(\n   596\u2192            instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n   597\u2192        ) -> None:\n   598\u2192            nonlocal new_offset\n   599\u2192            (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n   600\u2192            # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\n   601\u2192            (new_target,) = (\n   602\u2192                i2\n   603\u2192                for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\n   604\u2192                if i1 is target\n   605\u2192            )\n   606\u2192            assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\n   607\u2192            assert new_target.offset is not None\n   608\u2192            new_offset = new_target.offset\n   609\u2192\n   610\u2192        transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\n   611\u2192        assert new_offset >= 0\n   612\u2192\n   613\u2192        if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n   614\u2192            # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\n   615\u2192            # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\n   616\u2192            # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\n   617\u2192            # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\n   618\u2192            if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\n   619\u2192                block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\n   620\u2192                    new_offset\n   621\u2192                ] = {}\n   622\u2192\n   623\u2192                def remap_block_offsets(\n   624\u2192                    instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n   625\u2192                ) -> None:\n   626\u2192                    # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\n   627\u2192                    # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\n   628\u2192                    # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n   629\u2192                    # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\n   630\u2192                    # replaced.\n   631\u2192                    prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\n   632\u2192                    for inst in instructions:\n   633\u2192                        if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\n   634\u2192                            meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n   635\u2192                        ):\n   636\u2192                            break\n   637\u2192                        if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\":\n   638\u2192                            prefix_blocks.append(inst)\n   639\u2192\n   640\u2192                    # offsets into prefix\n   641\u2192                    for inst, o in zip(\n   642\u2192                        prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n   643\u2192                    ):\n   644\u2192                        block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\n   645\u2192\n   646\u2192                    # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\n   647\u2192                    old_start_offset = (\n   648\u2192                        cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\n   649\u2192                    )\n   650\u2192                    # offsets into old bytecode - include all offsets, not just > old_start_offset\n   651\u2192                    old_inst_offsets = sorted(setup_fn_target_offsets)\n   652\u2192                    targets = _filter_iter(\n   653\u2192                        instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\n   654\u2192                    )\n   655\u2192                    new_targets = _filter_iter(\n   656\u2192                        zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\n   657\u2192                        targets,\n   658\u2192                        lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\n   659\u2192                    )\n   660\u2192                    for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\n   661\u2192                        block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\n   662\u2192\n   663\u2192                transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\n   664\u2192\n   665\u2192            # remap all offsets in setup_fn_target_offsets\n   666\u2192            setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n   667\u2192                block_target_offset_remap.get(n, n)\n   668\u2192                for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n   669\u2192            )\n   670\u2192        return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\n   671\u2192            meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\n   672\u2192        )\n   673\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   240\u2192@dataclasses.dataclass\n   241\u2192class ResumeFunctionMetadata:\n   242\u2192    code: types.CodeType\n   243\u2192    instructions: list[Instruction] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\n   244\u2192    # Python 3.11+ fields\n   245\u2192    # NOTE: Python 3.11 removed blocks, but for our purposes, a \"block\" consists\n   246\u2192    # of instructions of all exception table entries that have the same target.\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192    # map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\n   249\u2192    prefix_block_target_offset_remap: list[int] = dataclasses.field(\n   250\u2192        default_factory=list\n   251\u2192    )\n   252\u2192    # per-offset map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\n   253\u2192    block_target_offset_remap: dict[int, dict[int, int]] = dataclasses.field(\n   254\u2192        default_factory=dict\n   255\u2192    )\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192def _filter_iter(\n   259\u2192    l1: Iterable[Any],\n   260\u2192    l2: Iterable[Any],\n   261\u2192    cond: Callable[[Any, Any], bool],\n   262\u2192) -> list[Any]:\n   263\u2192    \"\"\"\n   264\u2192    Two-pointer conditional filter.\n   265\u2192    e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\n   266\u2192    returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\n   267\u2192    \"\"\"\n   268\u2192    it = iter(l2)\n   269\u2192    res: list[Instruction] = []\n   270\u2192    try:\n   271\u2192        cur = next(it)\n   272\u2192        for val in l1:\n   273\u2192            if cond(val, cur):\n   274\u2192                res.append(val)\n   275\u2192                cur = next(it)\n   276\u2192    except StopIteration:\n   277\u2192        pass\n   278\u2192    return res\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192def _load_tuple_and_call(tup: tuple[Any, ...]) -> list[Instruction]:\n   282\u2192    insts: list[Instruction] = []\n   283\u2192    _initial_push_null(insts)\n   284\u2192    insts.extend(create_load_const(val) for val in tup)\n   285\u2192    insts.extend(create_call_function(len(tup), False))\n   286\u2192    return insts\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192class ContinueExecutionCache:\n   290\u2192    cache = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\n   291\u2192    generated_code_metadata = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192    @classmethod\n   294\u2192    def lookup(cls, code: types.CodeType, lineno: int, *key: Any) -> types.CodeType:\n   295\u2192        if code not in cls.cache:\n   296\u2192            cls.cache[code] = {}\n   297\u2192        key = tuple(key)\n   298\u2192        if key not in cls.cache[code]:\n   299\u2192            cls.cache[code][key] = cls.generate(code, lineno, *key)\n   300\u2192        return cls.cache[code][key]\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192    @classmethod\n   303\u2192    def generate(\n   304\u2192        cls,\n   305\u2192        code: types.CodeType,\n   306\u2192        lineno: int,\n   307\u2192        offset: int,\n   308\u2192        setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],  # only used in Python 3.11+\n   309\u2192        nstack: int,\n   310\u2192        argnames: tuple[str, ...],\n   311\u2192        argnames_null: tuple[str, ...],\n   312\u2192        setup_fns: tuple[ReenterWith, ...],\n   313\u2192        stack_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\n   314\u2192        argnames_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\n   315\u2192        null_idxes: tuple[int, ...],\n   316\u2192        # mainly used to ensure distinct code objects per stack trace,\n   317\u2192        # which prevents excessive recompilation of inner frames\n   318\u2192        nested_code_objs: tuple[types.CodeType],\n   319\u2192    ) -> types.CodeType:\n   320\u2192        assert offset is not None\n   321\u2192        assert not (\n   322\u2192            code.co_flags\n   323\u2192            & (CO_GENERATOR | CO_COROUTINE | CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE | CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR)\n   324\u2192        )\n   325\u2192        assert code.co_flags & CO_OPTIMIZED\n   326\u2192        if code in ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata:\n   327\u2192            return cls.generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n   328\u2192                code,\n   329\u2192                lineno,\n   330\u2192                offset,\n   331\u2192                setup_fn_target_offsets,\n   332\u2192                nstack,\n   333\u2192                argnames,\n   334\u2192                argnames_null,\n   335\u2192                setup_fns,\n   336\u2192                stack_ctx_vars,\n   337\u2192 sys.version_info >= (3, 11)\n   343\u2192        meta = ResumeFunctionMetadata(code)\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192        def update(\n   346\u2192            instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n   347\u2192        ) -> None:\n   348\u2192            meta.instructions = copy.deepcopy(instructions)\n   349\u2192\n   350\u2192            args = [\"__nested_resume_fns\", \"__nested_frame_values\"]\n   351\u2192            args += [f\"___stack{i}\" for i in range(nstack)]\n   352\u2192            args.extend(v for v in argnames if v not in args)\n   353\u2192            freevars = tuple(code_options[\"co_cellvars\"] or []) + tuple(\n   354\u2192                code_options[\"co_freevars\"] or []\n   355\u2192            )\n   356\u2192            freevars = tuple(sorted(freevars))\n   357\u2192            code_options[\"co_name\"] = (\n   358\u2192                f\"{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{code_options['co_name']}_at_{lineno}\"\n   359\u2192            )\n   360\u2192            if is_py311_plus:\n   361\u2192                qualified_path = code_options[\"co_qualname\"].rsplit(\".\", maxsplit=1)\n   362\u2192                if len(qualified_path) == 1:\n   363\u2192                    code_options[\"co_qualname\"] = code_options[\"co_name\"]\n   364\u2192                else:\n   365\u2192                    assert len(qualified_path) == 2\n   366\u2192                    module_name, co_name = qualified_path\n   367\u2192                    code_options[\"co_qualname\"] = (\n   368\u2192                        f\"{module_name}.{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{co_name}_at_{lineno}\"\n   369\u2192 freevars\n   373\u2192            code_options[\"co_argcount\"] = len(args)\n   374\u2192            code_options[\"co_posonlyargcount\"] = 0\n   375\u2192            code_options[\"co_kwonlyargcount\"] = 0\n   376\u2192            code_options[\"co_varnames\"] = tuple(\n   377\u2192                args\n   378\u2192                + [v for v in argnames_null if v not in args]\n   379\u2192                + [v for v in code_options[\"co_varnames\"] if v not in args]\n   380\u2192                + [IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME]\n   381\u2192            )\n   382\u2192            code_options[\"co_flags\"] = code_options[\"co_flags\"] & ~(\n   383\u2192                CO_VARARGS | CO_VARKEYWORDS\n   384\u2192            )\n   385\u2192            target = next(i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n   386\u2192\n   387\u2192            prefix = []\n   388\u2192            if is_py311_plus:\n   389\u2192                if freevars:\n   390\u2192                    prefix.append(\n   391\u2192                        create_instruction(\"COPY_FREE_VARS\", arg=len(freevars))\n   392\u2192                    )\n   393\u2192                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"RESUME\", arg=0))\n   394\u2192\n   395\u2192            # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue to prevent graph breaks.\n   396\u2192            # This doesn't really do anything at runtime, but dynamo will trace this\n   397\u2192            # and will know that we're in a resume function prologue.\n   398\u2192            prefix.extend(\n   399\u2192                [\n   400\u2192                    create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=True),\n   401\u2192                    create_instruction(\n   402\u2192                        \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n   403\u2192                    ),\n   404\u2192                ]\n   405\u2192            )\n   406\u2192\n   407\u2192            cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\n   408\u2192            hooks = {fn.stack_index: fn for fn in setup_fns}\n   409\u2192            hook_target_offsets = {\n   410\u2192                fn.stack_index: setup_fn_target_offsets[i]\n   411\u2192                for i, fn in enumerate(setup_fns)\n   412\u2192            }\n   413\u2192            offset_to_inst = {inst.offset: inst for inst in instructions}\n   414\u2192            # map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\n   415\u2192            old_hook_target_remap = {}\n   416\u2192            null_idxes_i = 0\n   417\u2192            stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\n   418\u2192            for i in range(nstack):\n   419\u2192                while (\n   420\u2192                    null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\n   421\u2192                    and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\n   422\u2192                ):\n   423\u2192                    prefix.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n   424\u2192                    null_idxes_i += 1\n   425\u2192                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=f\"___stack{i}\"))\n   426\u2192                if i in hooks:\n   427\u2192                    hook = hooks.pop(i)\n   428\u2192                    hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\n   429\u2192                    prefix.extend(hook_insts)\n   430\u2192                    if is_py311_plus:\n   431\u2192                        hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\n   432\u2192                        old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n   433\u2192                        meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n   434\u2192                        old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\n   435\u2192                if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\n   436\u2192                    # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\n   437\u2192                    # Load args for context variable and construct it\n   438\u2192                    prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192            if is_py311_plus:\n   441\u2192                # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\n   442\u2192                # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\n   443\u2192                meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\n   444\u2192                    reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\n   445\u2192                )\n   446\u2192\n   447\u2192            assert not hooks\n   448\u2192\n   449\u2192            # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\n   450\u2192            # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\n   451\u2192            for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\n   452\u2192                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=name))\n   453\u2192                prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\n   454\u2192                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=name))\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192            # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\n   457\u2192            if argnames_null:\n   458\u2192                assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\n   459\u2192                for v in argnames_null:\n   460\u2192                    assert v not in args\n   461\u2192                    prefix.extend(\n   462\u2192                        [\n   463\u2192                            create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"),\n   464\u2192                            create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=v),\n   465\u2192                        ]\n   466\u2192                    )\n   467\u2192\n   468\u2192            # Call nested resume function\n   469\u2192            if nested_code_objs:\n   470\u2192                prefix.extend(\n   471\u2192                    [\n   472\u2192                        # set up __nested_resume_fns[-1] call\n   473\u2192                        *add_push_null(\n   474\u2192                            [\n   475\u2192                   create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n   479\u2192                                create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n   480\u2192                            ]\n   481\u2192                        ),\n   482\u2192                        # del __nested_resume_fns[-1]\n   483\u2192                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n   484\u2192                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n   485\u2192                        create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n   486\u2192                        # load [__nested_resume_fns, __nested_frame_values]\n   487\u2192                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n   488\u2192                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n   489\u2192                        create_instruction(\"BUILD_LIST\", arg=2),\n   490\u2192                        # load __nested_frame_values[-1]\n   491\u2192                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n   492\u2192                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n   493\u2192                        create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n   494\u2192                        # create [\n   495\u2192                        #     __nested_resume_fns,\n   496\u2192                        #     __nested_frame_values,\n   497\u2192                        #     *__nested_frame_values[-1],\n   498\u2192 Output
"    98\u2192def _try_except_tf_mode_template(dummy: Any, stack_var_name: Any) -> None:\n    99\u2192    # NOTE: Make sure this name matches what is generated by symbolic_convert:import_source\n   100\u2192    # on torch._dynamo.utils.\n   101\u2192    global __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils\n   102\u2192    try:\n   103\u2192        dummy\n   104\u2192    except:  # noqa: E722, B001\n   105\u2192        __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils.set_torch_function_mode_stack(  # type: ignore[name-defined]\n   106\u2192            stack_var_name\n   107\u2192        )\n   108\u2192        raise\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\n   112\u2192class ReenterWith:\n   113\u2192    stack_index: int\n   114\u2192    target_values: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]] = None\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    def try_except_torch_function_mode(\n   117\u2192        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n   118\u2192    ) -> list[Instruction]:\n   119\u2192        \"\"\"\n   120\u2192        Codegen based off of:\n   121\u2192        try:\n   122\u2192            (rest)\n   123\u2192        except:\n   124\u2192            (restore previous tf mode stack)\n   125\u2192            raise\n   126\u2192        \"\"\"\n   127\u2192        from .variables.torch_function import get_prev_stack_var_name\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192        setup_try_except, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n   130\u2192            _try_except_tf_mode_template,\n   131\u2192            self.stack_index,\n   132\u2192            varname_map={\"stack_var_name\": get_prev_stack_var_name()},\n   133\u2192        )\n   134\u2192        cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192        return setup_try_except\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    # If we do not want to destroy the stack, we can do the same thing as a\n   139\u2192    # `SETUP_WITH` block, only that we store the context manager in a local_symbol\n   140\u2192    def try_finally(\n   141\u2192        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n   142\u2192    ) -> list[Instruction]:\n   143\u2192        \"\"\"\n   144\u2192        Codegen based off of:\n   145\u2192        load args\n   146\u2192        enter context\n   147\u2192        try:\n   148\u2192            (rest)\n   149\u2192        finally:\n   150\u2192            exit context\n   151\u2192        \"\"\"\n   152\u2192        # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\n   153\u2192        load_args = []\n   154\u2192        if self.target_values:\n   155\u2192            load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\n   156\u2192        ctx_name = unique_id(f\"___context_manager_{self.stack_index}\")\n   157\u2192        if ctx_name not in code_options[\"co_varnames\"]:\n   158\u2192            code_options[\"co_varnames\"] += (ctx_name,)\n   159\u2192        for name in [\"__enter__\", \"__exit__\"]:\n   160\u2192            if name not in code_options[\"co_names\"]:\n   161\u2192                code_options[\"co_names\"] += (name,)\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192        create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\n   164\u2192        _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\n   165\u2192        create_ctx.extend(\n   166\u2192            [\n   167\u2192                *load_args,\n   168\u2192                *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\n   169\u2192                create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=ctx_name),\n   170\u2192            ]\n   171\u2192        )\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192        def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\n   174\u2192            ctx.__enter__()\n   175\u2192            try:\n   176\u2192                dummy\n   177\u2192            finally:\n   178\u2192                ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192        setup_try_finally, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n   181\u2192            _template, self.stack_index, varname_map={\"ctx\": ctx_name}\n   182\u2192        )\n   183\u2192        cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n   184\u2192        return create_ctx + setup_try_finally\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192    def __call__(\n   187\u2192        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n   188\u2192    ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], Optional[Instruction]]:\n   189\u2192        \"\"\"\n   190\u2192        Codegen based off of:\n   191\u2192        with ctx(args):\n   192\u2192            (rest)\n   193\u2192        \"\"\"\n   194\u2192        # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\n   195\u2192        load_args = []\n   196\u2192        if self.target_values:\n   197\u2192            load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192        create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\n   200\u2192        _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\n   201\u2192        create_ctx.extend(\n   202\u2192            [\n   203\u2192                *load_args,\n   204\u2192                *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\n   205\u2192            ]\n   206\u2192        )\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192        def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\n   209\u2192            with ctx:\n   210\u2192                dummy\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192        setup_with, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n   213\u2192            _template, self.stack_index\n   214\u2192        )\n   215\u2192        cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192        load_fast_ctx_inst = next(\n   218\u2192            (\n   219\u2192                inst\n   220\u2192                for inst in setup_with\n   221\u2192                if inst.opname == \"LOAD_FAST\" and inst.argval == \"ctx\"\n   222\u2192            ),\n   223\u2192            None,\n   224\u2192        )\n   225\u2192        assert load_fast_ctx_inst is not None\n   226\u2192        # ctx already loaded on stack before the template - no need to LOAD_FAST\n   227\u2192        overwrite_instruction(load_fast_ctx_inst, [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192        # 3.11+ only\n   230\u2192        push_exc_info_gen = (\n   231\u2192            inst for inst in epilogue if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\"\n   232\u2192        )\n   233\u2192        push_exc_info_inst = next(push_exc_info_gen, None)\n   234\u2192        # expect only 1 PUSH_EXC_INFO in epilogue\n   235\u2192        assert next(push_exc_info_gen, None) is None\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192        return create_ctx + setup_with, push_exc_info_inst\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

121. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 50,
  "path": "torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: Core module responsible for converting Python bytecode into TorchDynamo's symbolic execution format.\\n3: \\n4: This module implements the bytecode-level tracing system that allows TorchDynamo to analyze\\n5: and transform Python code. It converts Python bytecode instructions into a symbolic format\\n6: that tracks the flow of tensors and other values through the program.\\n7: \\n8: Key components:\\n9: - InstructionTranslatorBase: Base class for converting bytecode to symbolic execution\\n10: - InstructionTranslator: Main translator for function bytecode\\n11: - InliningInstructionTranslator: Handles inlining of called functions\\n12: - SpeculationLog: Manages state for speculative execution and rollback\\n13: \\n14: The symbolic conversion process handles:\\n15: - Control flow (loops, conditionals, etc.)\\n16: - Function inlining and call stack management\\n17: - Tracking of program values and side effects\\n18: - Graph breaks and resumption points\\n19: - Exception handling and stack frame management\\n20: \\n21: This is a core part of TorchDynamo's tracing system that enables ahead-of-time\\n22: optimization of PyTorch programs.\\n23: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n24: \\n25: from __future__ import annotations\\n26: \\n27: import collections\\n28: import collections.abc\\n29: import contextlib\\n30: import copy\\n31: import dataclasses\\n32: import dis\\n33: import functools\\n34: import importlib\\n35: import inspect\\n36: import itertools\\n37: import linecache\\n38: import logging\\n39: import operator\\n40: import re\\n41: import sys\\n42: import threading\\n43: import traceback\\n44: import types\\n45: import weakref\\n46: from traceback import StackSummary\\n47: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, NoReturn, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\\n48: from typing_extensions import TypeAlias, TypeIs\\n49: from unittest.mock import patch\\n50: \\n51: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL1-50\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: Core module responsible for converting Python bytecode into TorchDynamo's symbolic execution format.\n3: \n4: This module implements the bytecode-level tracing system that allows TorchDynamo to analyze\n5: and transform Python code. It converts Python bytecode instructions into a symbolic format\n6: that tracks the flow of tensors and other values through the program.\n7: \n8: Key components:\n9: - InstructionTranslatorBase: Base class for converting bytecode to symbolic execution\n10: - InstructionTranslator: Main translator for function bytecode\n11: - InliningInstructionTranslator: Handles inlining of called functions\n12: - SpeculationLog: Manages state for speculative execution and rollback\n13: \n14: The symbolic conversion process handles:\n15: - Control flow (loops, conditionals, etc.)\n16: - Function inlining and call stack management\n17: - Tracking of program values and side effects\n18: - Graph breaks and resumption points\n19: - Exception handling and stack frame management\n20: \n21: This is a core part of TorchDynamo's tracing system that enables ahead-of-time\n22: optimization of PyTorch programs.\n23: \"\"\"\n24: \n25: from __future__ import annotations\n26: \n27: import collections\n28: import collections.abc\n29: import contextlib\n30: import copy\n31: import dataclasses\n32: import dis\n33: import functools\n34: import importlib\n35: import inspect\n36: import itertools\n37: import linecache\n38: import logging\n39: import operator\n40: import re\n41: import sys\n42: import threading\n43: import traceback\n44: import types\n45: import weakref\n46: from traceback import StackSummary\n47: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, NoReturn, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\n48: from typing_extensions import TypeAlias, TypeIs\n49: from unittest.mock import patch\n50: \n51: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL1-50"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"2467:     def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2468:         log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\\\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\\\")\\n2469:         if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\\n2470:             unimplemented_v2(\\n2471:                 gb_type=\\\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\\\",\\n2472:                 context=\\\"\\\",\\n2473:                 explanation=\\\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \\\"\\n2474:                 \\\"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\\\",\\n2475:                 hints=[],\\n2476:             )\\n2477:         all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\\n2478:             self,\\n2479:             reason=GraphCompileReason(\\\"store_attr\\\", [self.frame_summary()]),\\n2480:             stack_pops=2,\\n2481:         )\\n2482:         self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\\n2483:         self.popn(2)\\n2484:         self.output.add_output_instructions(\\n2485:             self.create_call_resume_at(\\n2486:                 self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\\n2487:             )\\n2488:         )\\n2489: \\n2490:     def DELETE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2491: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2467-2490\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2467:     def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2468:         log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\")\n2469:         if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n2470:             unimplemented_v2(\n2471:                 gb_type=\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\",\n2472:                 context=\"\",\n2473:                 explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \"\n2474:                 \"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n2475:                 hints=[],\n2476:             )\n2477:         all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n2478:             self,\n2479:             reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n2480:             stack_pops=2,\n2481:         )\n2482:         self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n2483:         self.popn(2)\n2484:         self.output.add_output_instructions(\n2485:             self.create_call_resume_at(\n2486:                 self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n2487:             )\n2488:         )\n2489: \n2490:     def DELETE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2491: ",

124. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3 << 'EOFPYTHON'\nimport urllib.request\nimport sys\n\n# Read from remote repository (this won't work, but shows intent)\n# For now, let's create a marker file\nwith open(\"/workspace/SYMBOLIC_CONVERT_FIX.md\", \"w\") as f:\n    f.write(\"\"\"# Fix for symbolic_convert.py\n\n## Location: store_attr_graph_break method (around line 2482)\n\n### Original Code:\n```python\nself.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n```\n\n### Fixed Code:\n```python\n# Create a deep copy of the instruction to ensure proper handling\ninst_copy = copy.deepcopy(inst)\n# Clear exception table entry since the graph break changes the bytecode structure\nif hasattr(inst_copy, 'exn_tab_entry'):\n    inst_copy.exn_tab_entry = None\nself.output.add_output_instructions([inst_copy])\n```\n\n## Rationale:\nWhen STORE_ATTR causes a graph break inside a `with` block:\n1. The instruction might have an exception table entry that references exception handlers\n2. When a graph break occurs, the bytecode structure changes\n3. The old exception table entry becomes invalid in the new code structure\n4. Using deepcopy ensures we don't modify the original instruction\n5. Clearing the exn_tab_entry prevents issues with invalid exception handler references\n\"\"\")\nprint(\"Created fix documentation\")\nEOFPYTHON\n"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "Created fix documentation"
}

126. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/fix_symbolic_convert.py << 'EOF'\n\"\"\"\nThis script applies the fix to symbolic_convert.py\nThe key change is in the store_attr_graph_break method:\n- Change from: self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n- Change to: Using deepcopy and handling exn_tab_entry properly\n\"\"\"\nimport sys\n\n# Read the file\nwith open('/workspace/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py', 'r') as f:\n    lines = f.readlines()\n\n# Find and fix the line\nfor i, line in enumerate(lines):\n    # Look for the STORE_ATTR graph break method\n    if 'def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:' in line:\n        print(f\"Found method at line {i+1}\")\n        # Now look for the copy.copy line within the next 30 lines\n        for j in range(i, min(i+30, len(lines))):\n            if 'self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])' in lines[j]:\n                print(f\"Found copy.copy at line {j+1}\")\n                # Replace the line\n                lines[j] = '        inst_copy = copy.deepcopy(inst)\\n'\n                # Add the exn_tab_entry clearing code\n                lines.insert(j+1, '        # Clear exception table entry since graph break changes bytecode structure\\n')\n                lines.insert(j+2, '        if hasattr(inst_copy, \"exn_tab_entry\") and inst_copy.exn_tab_entry is not None:\\n')\n                lines.insert(j+3, '            inst_copy.exn_tab_entry = None\\n')\n                lines.insert(j+4, '        self.output.add_output_instructions([inst_copy])\\n')\n                break\n        break\n\n# Write back\nwith open('/workspace/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py', 'w') as f:\n    f.writelines(lines)\nprint(\"Fixed symbolic_convert.py\")\nEOF\npython3 /tmp/fix_symbolic_convert.py\n"
}

return self.store_attr_graph_break(inst)\\n2443:         val, obj = self.popn(2)\\n2444: \\n2445:         if isinstance(obj, NNModuleVariable) and not isinstance(val, ConstantVariable):\\n2446:             # We don't allow side effects during export on non-constant values\\n2447:             # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1475\\n2448:             assert not self.export, (\\n2449:                 f\\\"Mutating module attribute {inst.argval} during export.\\\"\\n2450:             )\\n2451: \\n2452:         try:\\n2453:             BuiltinVariable(setattr).call_function(\\n2454: compile\\\", exc_info=True)\\n2463:             e.remove_from_stats()\\n2464:             e.add_to_stats(\\\"graph_break\\\")\\n2465:         speculation.fail_and_restart_analysis(self.error_on_graph_break)\\n2466: \\n2467:     def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2468:         log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\\\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\\\")\\n2469:         if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\\n2470:             unimplemented_v2(\\n2471:                 gb_type=\\\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\\\",\\n2472:                 context=\\\"\\\",\\n2473:                 explanation=\\\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \\\"\\n2474:                 \\\"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\\\",\\n2475:                 hints=[],\\n2476:             )\\n2477:         all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\\n2478:             self,\\n2479:             reason=GraphCompileReason(\\\"store_attr\\\", [self.frame_summary()]),\\n2480:             stack_pops=2,\\n2481:         )\\n2482:         self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\\n2483:         self.popn(2)\\n2484:         self.output.add_output_instructions(\\n2485:             self.create_call_resume_at(\\n2486:                 self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\\n2487:             )\\n2488:         )\\n2489: \\n2490:     def DELETE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2491:         obj = self.pop()\\n2492:         BuiltinVariable(delattr).call_function(\\n2493:             self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n2494:             [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval)],\\n2495:             {},\\n2496: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2439-2495\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2439:     def STORE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2440:         speculation = self.speculate()\n2441:         if speculation.failed(self):\n2442:             return self.store_attr_graph_break(inst)\n2443:         val, obj = self.popn(2)\n2444: \n2445:         if isinstance(obj, NNModuleVariable) and not isinstance(val, ConstantVariable):\n2446:             # We don't allow side effects during export on non-constant values\n2447:             # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1475\n2448:             assert not self.export, (\n2449:                 f\"Mutating module attribute {inst.argval} during export.\"\n2450:             )\n2451: \n2452:         try:\n2453:             BuiltinVariable(setattr).call_function(\n2454:                 self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n2455:                 [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval), val],\n2456:                 {},\n2457:             )\n2458:             return\n2459:         except Unsupported as e:\n2460:             if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n2461:                 raise\n2462:             log.debug(\"STORE_ATTR triggered compile\", exc_info=True)\n2463:             e.remove_from_stats()\n2464:             e.add_to_stats(\"graph_break\")\n2465:         speculation.fail_and_restart_analysis(self.error_on_graph_break)\n2466: \n2467:     def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2468:         log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\")\n2469:         if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n2470:             unimplemented_v2(\n2471:                 gb_type=\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\",\n2472:                 context=\"\",\n2473:                 explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \"\n2474:                 \"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n2475:                 hints=[],\n2476:             )\n2477:         all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n2478:             self,\n2479:             reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n2480:             stack_pops=2,\n2481:         )\n2482:         self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n2483:         self.popn(2)\n2484:         self.output.add_output_instructions(\n2485:             self.create_call_resume_at(\n2486:                 self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n2487:             )\n2488:         )\n2489: \n2490:     def DELETE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2491:         obj = self.pop()\n2492:         BuiltinVariable(delattr).call_function(\n2493:             self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n2494:             [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval)],\n2495:             {},\n2496: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2439-2495"
  }

131. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nPatch for symbolic_convert.py to fix STORE_ATTR exception table handling.\n\nThis script applies the necessary changes to torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py\nto properly handle exception table entries when STORE_ATTR causes a graph break\ninside with blocks.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport re\n\ndef apply_patch(file_path):\n    \"\"\"Apply the fix to symbolic_convert.py\"\"\"\n\n    with open(file_path, 'r') as f:\n        content = f.read()\n\n    # Find and replace the store_attr_graph_break method\n    # Look for the pattern: self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n    # in the context of the store_attr_graph_break method\n\n    old_code = '''    def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\")\n        if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\",\n                context=\"\",\n                explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \"\n                \"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n        all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n            self,\n            reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n            stack_pops=2,\n        )\n        self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n        self.popn(2)\n        self.output.add_output_instructions(\n            self.create_call_resume_at(\n                self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n            )\n        )'''\n\n    new_code = '''    def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\")\n        if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\",\n                context=\"\",\n                explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \"\n                \"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n        all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n            self,\n            reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n            stack_pops=2,\n        )\n        inst_copy = copy.deepcopy(inst)\n        # Clear exception table entry since graph break changes bytecode structure\n        if hasattr(inst_copy, 'exn_tab_entry') and inst_copy.exn_tab_entry is not None:\n            inst_copy.exn_tab_entry = None\n        self.output.add_output_instructions([inst_copy])\n        self.popn(2)\n        self.output.add_output_instructions(\n            self.create_call_resume_at(\n                self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n            )\n        )'''\n\n    if old_code in content:\n        content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)\n        print(f\"\u2713 Applied patch to {file_path}\")\n        with open(file_path, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(content)\n        return True\n    else:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Could not find pattern to patch in {file_path}\")\n        print(\"This is expected if the file needs to be fetched from remote first\")\n        return False\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    if len(sys.argv) > 1:\n        file_path = sys.argv[1]\n    else:\n        file_path = \"/workspace/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py\"\n\n    apply_patch(file_path)\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/PATCH_SYMBOLIC_CONVERT.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nPatch for symbolic_convert.py to fix STORE_ATTR exception table handling.\n\nThis script applies the necessary changes to torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py\nto properly handle exception table entries when STORE_ATTR causes a graph break\ninside with blocks.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport re\n\ndef apply_patch(file_path):\n    \"\"\"Apply the fix to symbolic_convert.py\"\"\"\n\n    with open(file_path, 'r') as f:\n        content = f.read()\n\n    # Find and replace the store_attr_graph_break method\n    # Look for the pattern: self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n    # in the context of the store_attr_graph_break method\n\n    old_code = '''    def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\")\n        if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\",\n                context=\"\",\n                explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \"\n                \"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n        all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n            self,\n            reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n            stack_pops=2,\n        )\n        self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n        self.popn(2)\n        self.output.add_output_instructions(\n            self.create_call_resume_at(\n                self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n            )\n        )'''\n\n    new_code = '''    def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\")\n        if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\",\n                context=\"\",\n                explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \"\n                \"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n        all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n            self,\n            reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n            stack_pops=2,\n        )\n        inst_copy = copy.deepcopy(inst)\n        # Clear exception table entry since graph break changes bytecode structure\n        if hasattr(inst_copy, 'exn_tab_entry') and inst_copy.exn_tab_entry is not None:\n            inst_copy.exn_tab_entry = None\n        self.output.add_output_instructions([inst_copy])\n        self.popn(2)\n        self.output.add_output_instructions(\n            self.create_call_resume_at(\n                self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n            )\n        )'''\n\n    if old_code in content:\n        content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)\n        print(f\"\u2713 Applied patch to {file_path}\")\n        with open(file_path, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(content)\n        return True\n    else:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Could not find pattern to patch in {file_path}\")\n        print(\"This is expected if the file needs to be fetched from remote first\")\n        return False\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    if len(sys.argv) > 1:\n        file_path = sys.argv[1]\n    else:\n        file_path = \"/workspace/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py\"\n\n    apply_patch(file_path)\n",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1829: \\n1830:     def test_with_on_graph_break_inst(self):\\n1831:         def reversible(x):\\n1832:             print(\\\"Hello world\\\")  # Cause graph break so inline fails\\n1833:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\\n1834: \\n1835:         def fn(x):\\n1836:             with torch.enable_grad():\\n1837:                 a = torch.sin(x)\\n1838:                 b = reversible(a)\\n1839:                 c = torch.sigmoid(b)\\n1840:                 c.sum().backward()\\n1841:                 return x.grad\\n1842: \\n1843:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1844:         x.grad = None\\n1845:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1846:             ref = fn(x)\\n1847: \\n1848:         x.grad = None\\n1849:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1850:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1851:             res = opt_fn(x)\\n1852:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1853: \\n1854:     def test_with_on_graph_break_nested(self):\\n1855:         def reversible(x):\\n1856:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()  # Cause graph break so inline fails\\n1857:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\\n1858: \\n1859:         def fn(x):\\n1860:             # nested context manager failed previously\\n1861:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1862:                 with torch.enable_grad():\\n1863:                     a = torch.sin(x)\\n1864:                     b = reversible(a)\\n1865:                     c = torch.sigmoid(b)\\n1866:                     c.sum().backward()\\n1867:                     return x.grad\\n1868: \\n1869:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1870:         x.grad = None\\n1871:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1872:             ref = fn(x)\\n1873: \\n1874:         x.grad = None\\n1875:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1876:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1877:             res = opt_fn(x)\\n1878:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1879: \\n1880:     # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1446\\n1881:     def test_grad_mode_carrying_correct_state_after_graph_break(self):\\n1882:         def fn(x):\\n1883:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1884:                 y = x * 3\\n1885:                 print(\\\"Break\\\")\\n1886:                 z = x + 2\\n1887:             return y, z\\n1888: \\n1889:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1890:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1891:         y, z = opt_fn(x)\\n1892:         self.assertFalse(y.requires_grad)\\n1893:         self.assertFalse(z.requires_grad)\\n1894: \\n1895:     def test_abc_setattr(self):\\n1896:         # Cause graph break so inline fails\n1857:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\n1858: \n1859:         def fn(x):\n1860:             # nested context manager failed previously\n1861:             with torch.no_grad():\n1862:                 with torch.enable_grad():\n1863:                     a = torch.sin(x)\n1864:                     b = reversible(a)\n1865:                     c = torch.sigmoid(b)\n1866:                     c.sum().backward()\n1867:                     return x.grad\n1868: \n1869:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n1870:         x.grad = None\n1871:         with torch.no_grad():\n1872:             ref = fn(x)\n1873: \n1874:         x.grad = None\n1875:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n1876:         with torch.no_grad():\n1877:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1878:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1879: \n1880:     #   "content": "{\"content\":\"1895:     def test_abc_setattr(self):\\n1896:         # tests that we correctly bail out of __setattr__ calls\\n1897: \\n1898:         # TODO: does not ensure ABC classes are correctly inferred as ClassVariables\\n1899:         # (doesn't test the fix for 'super()')\\n1900: \\n1901:         class BaseModule(torch.nn.Module, ABC):\\n1902:             def blah(self, x):\\n1903:                 return x + 1\\n1904: \\n1905:         class Derived(BaseModule):\\n1906:             def __setattr__(self, name, value) -> None:\\n1907:                 super().__setattr__(name, value)\\n1908: \\n1909:             def forward(self, x):\\n1910:                 # expect a graph break on __setattr__\\n1911:                 self.foo = 0\\n1912:                 return self.blah(x)\\n1913: \\n1914:             def blah(self, x):\\n1915:                 return super().blah(x)\\n1916: \\n1917:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1918:         mod = Derived()\\n1919:         opt_mod = torch.compile(mod, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1920:         opt_mod(x)\\n1921: \\n1922:         # Not sure what this test is testing. It was earlier graph breaking on\\n1923:         # __dict__, so the counter >= 2. With __dict__ support, there is no\\n1924:         # graph break.\\n1925:         self.assertGreaterEqual(torch._dynamo.utils.counters[\\\"frames\\\"][\\\"ok\\\"], 1)\\n1926:         self.assertGreaterEqual(torch._dynamo.utils.counters[\\\"frames\\\"][\\\"total\\\"], 1)\\n1927: \\n1928:     @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\\\"suppress_errors\\\", True)\\n1929:     def test_guard_fail_tensor_bool(self):\\n1930:         @torch._dynamo.disable(recursive=False)\\n1931:         def fn():\\n1932:             condition_shape = (5, 5)\\n1933:             dtypes = (torch.bool,)\\n1934:             shapes = (\\n1935:                 (),\\n1936:                 (5,),\\n1937:                 (1, 5),\\n1938:             )\\n1939: \\n1940:             tensors = [\\n1941:                 torch.empty(shape, dtype=dtype).fill_(17)\\n1942:                 for shape, dtype in itertools.product(shapes, dtypes)\\n1943:             ]\\n1944: \\n1945:             x_vals = (5.0, *tensors)\\n1946:             y_vals = (6.0, *tensors)\\n1947: \\n1948:             @torch._dynamo.disable\\n1949:             def get_expected(condition, x, y):\\n1950:                 x_np = x.cpu().numpy() if isinstance(x, torch.Tensor) else x\\n1951:                 y_np = y.cpu().numpy() if isinstance(y, torch.Tensor) else y\\n1952:                 return torch.from_numpy(\\n1953:                     np.where(condition.cpu().numpy(), x_np, y_np)\\n1954:                 ).to(common_dtype)\\n1955: \\n1956:             for x, y in zip(x_vals, y_vals):\\n1957:                 condition = torch.empty(*condition_shape, dtype=torch.bool).bernoulli_()\\n1958:                 common_dtype = torch.result_type(x, y)\\n1959: \\n1960:                 def check_equal(condition, x, y):\\n1961:                     # NumPy aggressively promotes to double, hence cast to output to correct dtype\\n1962:                     expected = get_expected(condition, x, y)\\n1963:                     result = torch.where(condition, x, y)\\n1964:                     assert torch.allclose(expected, result)\\n1965: \\n1966:                 check_equal(condition, x, y)\\n1967:                 check_equal(condition, y, x)\\n1968: \\n1969:         fn()\\n1970:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1971:         opt_fn()\\n1972: \\n1973:     def test_guard_fail_nested_tuple(self):\\n1974:         def fn(args):\\n1975:             return torch.ones(()), args[0] * 2\\n1976: \\n1977:         # This adds a tensor check on args[1][0] and args[1][1]\\n1978:         args1 = (torch.ones(1), (torch.ones(1), torch.ones(1)))\\n1979:         args2 = (torch.ones(1), torch.ones(1))\\n1980:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1981:         ref = opt_fn(args1)\\n1982:         res = opt_fn(args2)\\n1983: \\n1984:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1985: \\n1986:     def test_nullcontext1(self):\\n1987:         @torch.compile(fullgraph=True, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1988:         def fn(x, ctx):\\n1989:             x = x.sin()\\n1990:             with ctx:\\n1991:                 x = x.cos()\\n1992:             x = x.sin()\\n1993:             return x\\n1994: \\n1995:         y = torch.randn(10)\\n1996:         self.assertTrue(same(fn(y, contextlib.nullcontext()), y.sin().cos().sin()))\\n1997: \\n1998:     def test_nullcontext2(self):\\n1999:         @torch.compile(fullgraph=True, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n2000:         def fn(x, ctx):\\n2001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL1895-2000\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1895:     def test_abc_setattr(self):\n1896:         # tests that we correctly bail out of __setattr__ calls\n1897: \n1898:         # TODO: does not ensure ABC classes are correctly inferred as ClassVariables\n1899:         # (doesn't test the fix for 'super()')\n1900: \n1901:         class BaseModule(torch.nn.Module, ABC):\n1902:             def blah(self, x):\n1903:                 return x + 1\n1904: \n1905:         class Derived(BaseModule):\n1906:             def __setattr__(self, name, value) -> None:\n1907:                 super().__setattr__(name, value)\n1908: \n1909:             def forward(self, x):\n1910:                 # expect a graph break on __setattr__\n1911:                 self.foo = 0\n1912:                 return self.blah(x)\n1913: \n1914:             def blah(self, x):\n1915:                 return super().blah(x)\n1916: \n1917:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n1918:         mod = Derived()\n1919:         opt_mod = torch.compile(mod, backend=\"eager\")\n1920:         opt_mod(x)\n1921: \n1922:         # Not sure what this test is testing. It was earlier graph breaking on\n1923:         # __dict__, so the counter >= 2. With __dict__ support, there is no\n1924:         # graph break.\n1925:         self.assertGreaterEqual(torch._dynamo.utils.counters[\"frames\"][\"ok\"], 1)\n1926:         self.assertGreaterEqual(torch._dynamo.utils.counters[\"frames\"][\"total\"], 1)\n1927: \n1928:     @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"suppress_errors\", True)\n1929:     def test_guard_fail_tensor_bool(self):\n1930:         @torch._dynamo.disable(recursive=False)\n1931:         def fn():\n1932:             condition_shape = (5, 5)\n1933:             dtypes = (torch.bool,)\n1934:             shapes = (\n1935:                 (),\n1936:                 (5,),\n1937:                 (1, 5),\n1938:             )\n1939: \n1940:             tensors = [\n1941:                 torch.empty(shape, dtype=dtype).fill_(17)\n1942:                 for shape, dtype in itertools.product(shapes, dtypes)\n1943:             ]\n1944: \n1945:             x_vals = (5.0, *tensors)\n1946:             y_vals = (6.0, *tensors)\n1947: \n1948:             @torch._dynamo.disable\n1949:             def get_expected(condition, x, y):\n1950:                 x_np = x.cpu().numpy() if isinstance(x, torch.Tensor) else x\n1951:                 y_np = y.cpu().numpy() if isinstance(y, torch.Tensor) else y\n1952:                 return torch.from_numpy(\n1953:                     np.where(condition.cpu().numpy(), x_np, y_np)\n1954:                 ).to(common_dtype)\n1955: \n1956:             for x, y in zip(x_vals, y_vals):\n1957:                 condition = torch.empty(*condition_shape, dtype=torch.bool).bernoulli_()\n1958:                 common_dtype = torch.result_type(x, y)\n1959: \n1960:                 def check_equal(condition, x, y):\n1961:                     # NumPy aggressively promotes to double, hence cast to output to correct dtype\n1962:                     expected = get_expected(condition, x, y)\n1963:                     result = torch.where(condition, x, y)\n1964:                     assert torch.allclose(expected, result)\n1965: \n1966:                 check_equal(condition, x, y)\n1967:                 check_equal(condition, y, x)\n1968: \n1969:         fn()\n1970:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n1971:         opt_fn()\n1972: \n1973:     def test_guard_fail_nested_tuple(self):\n1974:         def fn(args):\n1975:             return torch.ones(()), args[0] * 2\n1976: \n1977:         # This adds a tensor check on args[1][0] and args[1][1]\n1978:         args1 = (torch.ones(1), (torch.ones(1), torch.ones(1)))\n1979:         args2 = (torch.ones(1), torch.ones(1))\n1980:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n1981:         ref = opt_fn(args1)\n1982:         res = opt_fn(args2)\n1983: \n1984:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1985: \n1986:     def test_nullcontext1(self):\n1987:         @torch.compile(fullgraph=True, backend=\"eager\")\n1988:         def fn(x, ctx):\n1989:             x = x.sin()\n1990:             with ctx:\n1991:                 x = x.cos()\n1992:             x = x.sin()\n1993:             return x\n1994: \n1995:         y = torch.randn(10)\n1996:         self.assertTrue(same(fn(y, contextlib.nullcontext()), y.sin().cos().sin()))\n1997: \n1998:     def test_nullcontext2(self):\n1999:         @torch.compile(fullgraph=True, backend=\"eager\")\n2000:         def fn(x, ctx):\n2001: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL1895-2000"
  }

test if a nonlocal actually gets propagated\\n1906:         z = 0\\n1907:         k = 0\\n1908: \\n1909:         def create_ctx():\\n1910:             @contextmanager\\n1911:             def ctx(x):\\n1912:                 nonlocal z\\n1913:                 nonlocal k\\n1914:                 try:\\n1915:                     k = 100\\n1916:                     yield x.sin()\\n1917:                 finally:\\n1918:                     pass\\n1919: \\n1920:             return ctx\\n1921: \\n1922:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\\n1923:             nonlocal z\\n1924:             with ctx(x) as y:\\n1925:                 z = k\\n1926:                 return y.cos()\\n1927: \\n1928:         @torch.compile(backend=\\\"eager\\\", fullgraph=True)\\n1929:         def fn(x):\\n1930:             ctx = create_ctx()\\n1931:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\\n1932: \\n1933:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\\n1934:         y = fn(x)\\n1935:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\\n1936:         self.assertEqual(z, 100)\\n1937:         self.assertEqual(k, 100)\\n1938: \\n1939:     def test_change_parent_nonlocal_1(self):\\n1940:         # test if finally is executed and it is reading the correct variable\\n1941:         z = 1\\n1942:         k = 2\\n1943: \\n1944:         def create_ctx():\\n1945:             @contextmanager\\n1946:             def ctx(x):\\n1947:                 nonlocal z\\n1948:                 nonlocal k\\n1949:                 try:\\n1950:                     yield x.sin()\\n1951:                 finally:\\n1952:                     k = z\\n1953: \\n1954:             return ctx\\n1955: \\n1956:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\\n1957:             nonlocal z\\n1958:             z = 100\\n1959:             with ctx(x) as y:\\n1960:                 return y.cos()\\n1961: \\n1962:         @torch.compile(backend=\\\"eager\\\", fullgraph=True)\\n1963:         def fn(x):\\n1964:             ctx = create_ctx()\\n1965:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\\n1966: \\n1967:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\\n1968:         y = fn(x)\\n1969:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\\n1970:         self.assertEqual(z, 100)\\n1971:         self.assertEqual(k, 100)\\n1972: \\n1973:     def test_globals_change_in_other_file(self):\\n1974:         @contextmanager\\n1975:         def update_global_ctx():\\n1976:             global _variable, _variable1\\n1977:             try:\\n1978:                 _variable += 1\\n1979:                 _variable1 += 1\\n1980:                 yield\\n1981:             finally:\\n1982:                 pass\\n1983: \\n1984:         @torch.compile(backend=\\\"eager\\\", fullgraph=True)\\n1985:         def fn(x):\\n1986:             with update_global_ctx():\\n1987:                 pass\\n1988: \\n1989:             with test_functions.update_global_ctx(x) as a:\\n1990:                 # Ensure that the updated global values are read\\n1991:                 test_functions.constant3(2, 3)\\n1992:                 return x * a * (_variable + _variable1 + test_functions._variable)\\n1993: \\n1994:         res = fn(torch.ones(10))\\n1995:         self.assertEqual(_variable, 1)\\n1996:         self.assertEqual(_variable1, 1)\\n1997:         # Ensure that the reconstructed bytecode updates the global value in the\\n1998:         # other file.\\n1999:         self.assertEqual(test_functions._variable, 1)\\n2000:         self.assertEqual(res, 3 * torch.ones(10))\\n2001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py%3FL1900-2000\"}",

    "content": "1900:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n1901:         y = fn(x)\n1902:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n1903: \n1904:     def test_change_parent_nonlocal_0(self):\n1905:         # test if a nonlocal actually gets propagated\n1906:         z = 0\n1907:         k = 0\n1908: \n1909:         def create_ctx():\n1910:             @contextmanager\n1911:             def ctx(x):\n1912:                 nonlocal z\n1913:                 nonlocal k\n1914:                 try:\n1915:                     k = 100\n1916:                     yield x.sin()\n1917:                 finally:\n1918:                     pass\n1919: \n1920:             return ctx\n1921: \n1922:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n1923:             nonlocal z\n1924:             with ctx(x) as y:\n1925:                 z = k\n1926:                 return y.cos()\n1927: \n1928:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1929:         def fn(x):\n1930:             ctx = create_ctx()\n1931:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n1932: \n1933:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n1934:         y = fn(x)\n1935:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n1936:         self.assertEqual(z, 100)\n1937:         self.assertEqual(k, 100)\n1938: \n1939:     def test_change_parent_nonlocal_1(self):\n1940:         # test if finally is executed and it is reading the correct variable\n1941:         z = 1\n1942:         k = 2\n1943: \n1944:         def create_ctx():\n1945:             @contextmanager\n1946:             def ctx(x):\n1947:                 nonlocal z\n1948:                 nonlocal k\n1949:                 try:\n1950:                     yield x.sin()\n1951:                 finally:\n1952:                     k = z\n1953: \n1954:             return ctx\n1955: \n1956:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n1957:             nonlocal z\n1958:             z = 100\n1959:             with ctx(x) as y:\n1960:                 return y.cos()\n1961: \n1962:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1963:         def fn(x):\n1964:             ctx = create_ctx()\n1965:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n1966: \n1967:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n1968:         y = fn(x)\n1969:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n1970:         self.assertEqual(z, 100)\n1971:         self.assertEqual(k, 100)\n1972: \n1973:     def test_globals_change_in_other_file(self):\n1974:         @contextmanager\n1975:         def update_global_ctx():\n1976:             global _variable, _variable1\n1977:             try:\n1978:                 _variable += 1\n1979:                 _variable1 += 1\n1980:                 yield\n1981:             finally:\n1982:                 pass\n1983: \n1984:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1985:         def fn(x):\n1986:             with update_global_ctx():\n1987:                 pass\n1988: \n1989:             with test_functions.update_global_ctx(x) as a:\n1990:                 # Ensure that the updated global values are read\n1991:                 test_functions.constant3(2, 3)\n1992:                 return x * a * (_variable + _variable1 + test_functions._variable)\n1993: \n1994:         res = fn(torch.ones(10))\n1995:         self.assertEqual(_variable, 1)\n1996:         self.assertEqual(_variable1, 1)\n1997:         # Ensure that the reconstructed bytecode updates the global value in the\n1998:         # other file.\n1999:         self.assertEqual(test_functions._variable, 1)\n2000:         self.assertEqual(res, 3 * torch.ones(10))\n2001: ",
